title.zh: 卷二十五上 郊祀志
title.raw: 卷二十五上 郊祀志 Volume 25a: Treatise on Sacrifices 1

sentences.zh: 洪範八政，三曰祀。

translations.literal: In the Great Plan's eight policies, the third is sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: In the Great Plan's eight policies, the third is sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 祀者，所以昭孝事祖，通神明也。

translations.literal: Sacrifice makes filial devotion to ancestors manifest and communicates with spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Sacrifice makes filial devotion to ancestors manifest and communicates with spirits.

sentences.zh: 旁及四夷，莫不修之；

translations.literal: Its reach extended even to the four border peoples, none of whom failed to practice it.
translations.idiomatic: Its reach extended even to the four border peoples, none of whom failed to practice it.

sentences.zh: 下至禽獸，豺獺有祭。

translations.literal: Even among birds and beasts, wolves and otters have their offerings.
translations.idiomatic: Even among birds and beasts, wolves and otters have their offerings.

sentences.zh: 是以聖王為之典禮。

translations.literal: Therefore sage kings established ritual codes for it.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore sage kings established ritual codes for it.

sentences.zh: 民之精爽不貳，齊肅聰明者，神或降之，在男曰覡，在女曰巫，使制神之處位，為之牲器。

translations.literal: Among the people, those whose vital spirit was single-minded, pure, reverent, keen, and perceptive were sometimes granted access by spirits: men were called xi, women wu; they determined the places and ranks of spirits and prepared victims and vessels for them.
translations.idiomatic: Among the people, those whose vital spirit was single-minded, pure, reverent, keen, and perceptive were sometimes granted access by spirits: men were called xi, women wu; they determined the places and ranks of spirits and prepared victims and vessels for them.

sentences.zh: 使先聖之後，能知山川，敬於禮儀，明神之事者，以為祝；

translations.literal: Descendants of former sages who understood mountains and rivers, respected ritual forms, and comprehended matters of spirits were appointed invocators.
translations.idiomatic: Descendants of former sages who understood mountains and rivers, respected ritual forms, and comprehended matters of spirits were appointed invocators.

sentences.zh: 能知四時犧牲，壇場上下，氏姓所出者，以為宗。

translations.literal: Those who knew seasonal victims, altar arrangements, and clan origins were appointed lineage priests.
translations.idiomatic: Those who knew seasonal victims, altar arrangements, and clan origins were appointed lineage priests.

sentences.zh: 故有神民之官，各司其序，不相亂也。

translations.literal: Thus there were officials for spirits and officials for people, each managing its own order without confusion.
translations.idiomatic: Thus there were officials for spirits and officials for people, each managing its own order without confusion.

sentences.zh: 民神異業，敬而不黷，故神降之嘉生，民以物序，災禍不至，所求不匱。

translations.literal: Human and spirit affairs were kept distinct; people were reverent without irreverence. So spirits sent down good harvests, people had orderly goods, disasters did not arrive, and what they sought was not lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Human and spirit affairs were kept distinct; people were reverent without irreverence. So spirits sent down good harvests, people had orderly goods, disasters did not arrive, and what they sought was not lacking.

sentences.zh: 及少昊之衰，九黎亂德，民神雜擾，不可放物。

translations.literal: By the decline of Shaohao, however, the Nine Li threw virtue into chaos, and human and spirit affairs became mixed and disordered, beyond regulation.
translations.idiomatic: By the decline of Shaohao, however, the Nine Li threw virtue into chaos, and human and spirit affairs became mixed and disordered, beyond regulation.

sentences.zh: 家為巫史，享祀無度，黷齊明而神弗蠲。

translations.literal: Every household became shamans and scribes; sacrifices were made without measure, profaning purity and clarity so spirits no longer blessed them.
translations.idiomatic: Every household became shamans and scribes; sacrifices were made without measure, profaning purity and clarity so spirits no longer blessed them.

sentences.zh: 嘉生不降，禍災荐臻，莫盡其氣。

translations.literal: Good growth no longer descended, and calamities came one after another, exhausting people utterly.
translations.idiomatic: Good growth no longer descended, and calamities came one after another, exhausting people utterly.

sentences.zh: 顓頊受之，乃命南正重司天以屬神，命火正黎司地以屬民，使復舊常，亡相侵黷。

translations.literal: When Zhuanxu inherited this, he ordered Chong, Director of the South, to govern Heaven and attach to spirits, and Li, Director of Fire, to govern Earth and attach to people, restoring old norms so the two spheres no longer intruded upon each other.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhuanxu inherited this, he ordered Chong, Director of the South, to govern Heaven and attach to spirits, and Li, Director of Fire, to govern Earth and attach to people, restoring old norms so the two spheres no longer intruded upon each other.

sentences.zh: 自共工氏霸九州，其子曰句龍，能平水土，死為社祠。

translations.literal: From the time the Gonggong clan dominated the Nine Provinces, his son Goulong was able to regulate waters and soils; after death he became the spirit of Earth.
translations.idiomatic: From the time the Gonggong clan dominated the Nine Provinces, his son Goulong was able to regulate waters and soils; after death he became the spirit of Earth.

sentences.zh: 有烈山氏王天下，其子曰柱，能殖百穀，死為稷祠。

translations.literal: When the Lieshan clan ruled the realm, his son Zhu could cultivate the hundred grains; after death he became the spirit of Millet.
translations.idiomatic: When the Lieshan clan ruled the realm, his son Zhu could cultivate the hundred grains; after death he became the spirit of Millet.

sentences.zh: 故郊祀社稷，所從來尚矣。

translations.literal: Hence suburban sacrifices to Earth and Millet have very ancient origins.
translations.idiomatic: Hence suburban sacrifices to Earth and Millet have very ancient origins.

sentences.zh: 《虞書》曰，舜在璿璣玉衡，以齊七政。

translations.literal: The Book of Yu says: Shun examined the jeweled armillary and jade balance to align the seven celestial regulators.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Yu says: Shun examined the jeweled armillary and jade balance to align the seven celestial regulators.

sentences.zh: 遂類于上帝，禋于六宗，望秩于山川，遍于群神。

translations.literal: He then performed the lei sacrifice to the High God, the yin rite to the six honored powers, ranked distant offerings to mountains and rivers, and extended rites to all spirits.
translations.idiomatic: He then performed the lei sacrifice to the High God, the yin rite to the six honored powers, ranked distant offerings to mountains and rivers, and extended rites to all spirits.

sentences.zh: 揖五瑞，擇吉月日，見四嶽諸牧，班瑞。

translations.literal: He gathered the five tally-jades, chose auspicious month and day, met the Four Peaks and regional leaders, and redistributed the jades.
translations.idiomatic: He gathered the five tally-jades, chose auspicious month and day, met the Four Peaks and regional leaders, and redistributed the jades.

sentences.zh: 歲二月，東巡狩，至于岱宗。

translations.literal: In the second month of the year, he made an eastern inspection tour, arriving at Dai Peak.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the year, he made an eastern inspection tour, arriving at Dai Peak.

sentences.zh: 岱宗，泰山也。

translations.literal: Dai Peak is Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: Dai Peak is Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 柴，望秩于山川。

translations.literal: He burned firewood and offered ranked distant sacrifices to mountains and rivers.
translations.idiomatic: He burned firewood and offered ranked distant sacrifices to mountains and rivers.

sentences.zh: 遂見東后。

translations.literal: Then he met the eastern lords.
translations.idiomatic: Then he met the eastern lords.

sentences.zh: 東后者，諸侯也。

translations.literal: The eastern lords were the feudal rulers.
translations.idiomatic: The eastern lords were the feudal rulers.

sentences.zh: 合時月正日，同律度量衡，修五禮五樂，三帛二生一死為贄。

translations.literal: He harmonized seasons, months, and proper days; standardized pitch, measures, and weights; revised the five rites and five musics; and accepted tribute gifts of three silks, two live animals, and one dead one.
translations.idiomatic: He harmonized seasons, months, and proper days; standardized pitch, measures, and weights; revised the five rites and five musics; and accepted tribute gifts of three silks, two live animals, and one dead one.

sentences.zh: 五月，巡狩至南嶽。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, the inspection tour reached the Southern Peak.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month, the inspection tour reached the Southern Peak.

sentences.zh: 南嶽者，衡山也。

translations.literal: The Southern Peak is Mount Heng.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Peak is Mount Heng.

sentences.zh: 八月，巡狩至西嶽。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, the inspection tour reached the Western Peak.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month, the inspection tour reached the Western Peak.

sentences.zh: 西嶽者，華山也。

translations.literal: The Western Peak is Mount Hua.
translations.idiomatic: The Western Peak is Mount Hua.

sentences.zh: 十一月，巡狩至北嶽。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the inspection tour reached the Northern Peak.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month, the inspection tour reached the Northern Peak.

sentences.zh: 北嶽者，恆山也。

translations.literal: The Northern Peak is Mount Heng.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Peak is Mount Heng.

sentences.zh: 皆如岱宗之禮。

translations.literal: At all of them, the rites were as at Dai Peak.
translations.idiomatic: At all of them, the rites were as at Dai Peak.

sentences.zh: 中嶽，嵩高也。

translations.literal: The Central Peak is Songgao.
translations.idiomatic: The Central Peak is Songgao.

sentences.zh: 五載一巡狩。

translations.literal: One inspection tour was made every five years.
translations.idiomatic: One inspection tour was made every five years.

sentences.zh: 禹遵之。

translations.literal: Yu followed this practice.
translations.idiomatic: Yu followed this practice.

sentences.zh: 後十三世，至帝孔甲，淫德好神，神黷，二龍去之。

translations.literal: Thirteen generations later, under Emperor Kongjia, virtue became licentious and spirits were courted improperly; when the spirits were profaned, two dragons left him.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen generations later, under Emperor Kongjia, virtue became licentious and spirits were courted improperly; when the spirits were profaned, two dragons left him.

sentences.zh: 其後十三世，湯伐桀，欲讓夏社，不可，作夏社。

translations.literal: Thirteen generations after that, Tang overthrew Jie and wished to yield precedence to the Xia altar of Earth, but this was not accepted, so he established a Xia Earth altar.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen generations after that, Tang overthrew Jie and wished to yield precedence to the Xia altar of Earth, but this was not accepted, so he established a Xia Earth altar.

sentences.zh: 乃讓烈山子柱，而以周棄代為稷祠。

translations.literal: He then yielded precedence from Lieshan's son Zhu and replaced him with Zhou's Qi as the Millet spirit.
translations.idiomatic: He then yielded precedence from Lieshan's son Zhu and replaced him with Zhou's Qi as the Millet spirit.

sentences.zh: 後八世，帝太戊有桑穀生於廷，一暮大拱，懼。

translations.literal: Eight generations later, under Emperor Taiwu, mulberry and grain plants grew in the court and in one evening reached great girth, causing fear.
translations.idiomatic: Eight generations later, under Emperor Taiwu, mulberry and grain plants grew in the court and in one evening reached great girth, causing fear.

sentences.zh: 伊陟曰：「祅不勝德。」

translations.literal: Yi Zhi said, 'Portents cannot overcome virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zhi said, 'Portents cannot overcome virtue.'

sentences.zh: 太戊修德，桑穀死。

translations.literal: Taiwu cultivated virtue, and the strange mulberry and grain withered.
translations.idiomatic: Taiwu cultivated virtue, and the strange mulberry and grain withered.

sentences.zh: 伊陟贊巫咸。

translations.literal: Yi Zhi then supported Wu Xian.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Zhi then supported Wu Xian.

sentences.zh: 後十三世，帝武丁得傅說為相，殷復興焉，稱高宗。

translations.literal: Thirteen generations later, Emperor Wuding obtained Fu Yue as minister; Yin revived and Wuding was called Gaozong.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen generations later, Emperor Wuding obtained Fu Yue as minister; Yin revived and Wuding was called Gaozong.

sentences.zh: 有雉登鼎耳而雊，武丁懼。

translations.literal: A pheasant landed on the handle of a cauldron and crowed, and Wuding was afraid.
translations.idiomatic: A pheasant landed on the handle of a cauldron and crowed, and Wuding was afraid.

sentences.zh: 祖己曰：「修德。」

translations.literal: Zu Ji said, 'Cultivate virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: Zu Ji said, 'Cultivate virtue.'

sentences.zh: 武丁從之，位以永寧。

translations.literal: Wuding followed this, and his position remained in lasting peace.
translations.idiomatic: Wuding followed this, and his position remained in lasting peace.

sentences.zh: 後五世，帝乙嫚神而震死。

translations.literal: Five generations later, Emperor Yi slighted the spirits and died from a thunder strike.
translations.idiomatic: Five generations later, Emperor Yi slighted the spirits and died from a thunder strike.

sentences.zh: 後三世，帝紂淫亂，武王伐之。

translations.literal: Three generations later, Emperor Zhou was licentious and chaotic, and King Wu attacked him.
translations.idiomatic: Three generations later, Emperor Zhou was licentious and chaotic, and King Wu attacked him.

sentences.zh: 由是觀之，始未嘗不肅祇，後稍怠嫚也。

translations.literal: From this we see: at first rulers were always reverent toward spirits, but later gradually grew lax and contemptuous.
translations.idiomatic: From this we see: at first rulers were always reverent toward spirits, but later gradually grew lax and contemptuous.

sentences.zh: 周公相成王，王道大洽，制禮作樂，天子曰明堂辟雍，諸侯曰泮宮。

translations.literal: When the Duke of Zhou assisted King Cheng, royal governance was greatly harmonized; rites were systematized and music created. The Son of Heaven's institutions were called Mingtang and Biyong, and the feudal lords' schools were called Pangong.
translations.idiomatic: When the Duke of Zhou assisted King Cheng, royal governance was greatly harmonized; rites were systematized and music created. The Son of Heaven's institutions were called Mingtang and Biyong, and the feudal lords' schools were called Pangong.

sentences.zh: 郊祀后稷以配天，宗祀文王於明堂以配上帝。

translations.literal: In suburban sacrifice they honored Houji to match with Heaven; in ancestral temple sacrifice at the Mingtang they honored King Wen to match with the High God.
translations.idiomatic: In suburban sacrifice they honored Houji to match with Heaven; in ancestral temple sacrifice at the Mingtang they honored King Wen to match with the High God.

sentences.zh: 四海之內各以其職來助祭。

translations.literal: From within the four seas, each office came to assist in sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: From within the four seas, each office came to assist in sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 天子祭天下名山大川，懷柔百神，咸秩無文。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven sacrificed to the realm's famous mountains and great rivers, cherishing and pacifying the hundred spirits, each ranked without neglect.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven sacrificed to the realm's famous mountains and great rivers, cherishing and pacifying the hundred spirits, each ranked without neglect.

sentences.zh: 五嶽視三公，四瀆視諸侯。

translations.literal: The Five Peaks were treated at the level of the Three Excellencies, and the Four Waterways at the level of feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Peaks were treated at the level of the Three Excellencies, and the Four Waterways at the level of feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 而諸侯祭其疆內名山大川，大夫祭門、戶、井、灶、中霤五祀。

translations.literal: Feudal lords sacrificed to famous mountains and great rivers within their borders; great officers sacrificed to the Five Household Spirits of gate, door, well, hearth, and central drain.
translations.idiomatic: Feudal lords sacrificed to famous mountains and great rivers within their borders; great officers sacrificed to the Five Household Spirits of gate, door, well, hearth, and central drain.

sentences.zh: 士庶人祖考而已。

translations.literal: Scholars and commoners sacrificed only to their ancestors.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars and commoners sacrificed only to their ancestors.

sentences.zh: 各有典禮，而淫祀有禁。

translations.literal: Each rank had its proper rites, and illicit sacrifices were forbidden.
translations.idiomatic: Each rank had its proper rites, and illicit sacrifices were forbidden.

sentences.zh: 後十三世，世益衰，禮樂廢。

translations.literal: Thirteen generations later, the age grew ever weaker and rites and music fell into ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen generations later, the age grew ever weaker and rites and music fell into ruin.

sentences.zh: 幽王無道，為犬戎所敗，平王東徙雒邑。

translations.literal: King You was without the Way, was defeated by the Quanrong, and King Ping moved east to Luoyi.
translations.idiomatic: King You was without the Way, was defeated by the Quanrong, and King Ping moved east to Luoyi.

sentences.zh: 秦襄公攻戎救周，列為諸侯，而居西，自以為主少昊之神，作西畤，祠白帝，其牲用騮駒黃牛羝羊各一云。

translations.literal: Duke Xiang of Qin attacked the Rong and rescued Zhou, was enfeoffed among the lords, and dwelt in the west. Claiming to preside over Shaohao's deity, he established the Western altar and sacrificed to the White Thearch, using one red colt, one yellow ox, and one ram.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Xiang of Qin attacked the Rong and rescued Zhou, was enfeoffed among the lords, and dwelt in the west. Claiming to preside over Shaohao's deity, he established the Western altar and sacrificed to the White Thearch, using one red colt, one yellow ox, and one ram.

sentences.zh: 其後十四年，秦文公東獵汧渭之間，卜居之而吉。

translations.literal: Fourteen years later, Duke Wen of Qin hunted east between the Qian and Wei and divined the site as auspicious for settlement.
translations.idiomatic: Fourteen years later, Duke Wen of Qin hunted east between the Qian and Wei and divined the site as auspicious for settlement.

sentences.zh: 文公

translations.literal: Duke Wen
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen

sentences.zh: 薨黃蛇自天下屬地，其口止於鄜衍。

translations.literal: died, and a yellow snake descended from Heaven to the ground, with its mouth ending at Fuyan.
translations.idiomatic: died, and a yellow snake descended from Heaven to the ground, with its mouth ending at Fuyan.

sentences.zh: 文公問史敦，敦曰：「此上帝之徵，君其祠之。」

translations.literal: Duke Wen asked the historian Dun, who said, 'This is a sign from the High God; you should sacrifice to it.'
translations.idiomatic: Duke Wen asked the historian Dun, who said, 'This is a sign from the High God; you should sacrifice to it.'

sentences.zh: 於是作鄜畤，用三牲郊祭白帝焉。

translations.literal: So he established the Fu altar and used three sacrificial animals for a suburban rite to the White Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: So he established the Fu altar and used three sacrificial animals for a suburban rite to the White Thearch.

sentences.zh: 自未作鄜畤，而雍旁故有吳陽武畤，雍東有好畤，皆廢無祀。

translations.literal: Before the Fu altar was created, there had long been the Wuyang Wu altar near Yong and the Hao altar east of Yong, both abandoned and unsacrificed.
translations.idiomatic: Before the Fu altar was created, there had long been the Wuyang Wu altar near Yong and the Hao altar east of Yong, both abandoned and unsacrificed.

sentences.zh: 或曰：「自古以雍州積高，神明之隩，故立畤郊上帝，諸神祠皆聚云。

translations.literal: Some said, 'Since ancient times Yongzhou has high accumulated terrain and is a hidden seat of numinous powers; therefore altars were set up in the suburbs to the High God, and all spirit shrines gathered there.
translations.idiomatic: Some said, 'Since ancient times Yongzhou has high accumulated terrain and is a hidden seat of numinous powers; therefore altars were set up in the suburbs to the High God, and all spirit shrines gathered there.

sentences.zh: 蓋黃帝時嘗用事，雖晚周亦郊焉。」

translations.literal: Likely the Yellow Thearch once performed rites there, and even in late Zhou suburban rites were still held there.'
translations.idiomatic: Likely the Yellow Thearch once performed rites there, and even in late Zhou suburban rites were still held there.'

sentences.zh: 其語不經見，縉紳者弗道。

translations.literal: But these statements are not attested in classic sources, and the gentry did not transmit them.
translations.idiomatic: But these statements are not attested in classic sources, and the gentry did not transmit them.

sentences.zh: 作鄜畤後九年，文公獲若石云，于陳倉北阪城祠之。

translations.literal: Nine years after establishing the Fu altar, Duke Wen obtained a stone-like object and sacrificed to it at a shrine in the northern slope fortress of Chencang.
translations.idiomatic: Nine years after establishing the Fu altar, Duke Wen obtained a stone-like object and sacrificed to it at a shrine in the northern slope fortress of Chencang.

sentences.zh: 其神或歲不至，或歲數。

translations.literal: Its spirit sometimes did not arrive for a year, and sometimes came several times in a year.
translations.idiomatic: Its spirit sometimes did not arrive for a year, and sometimes came several times in a year.

sentences.zh: 來也常以夜，光輝若流星，從東方來，集於祠城，若雄雉，其聲殷殷云，野雞夜鳴。

translations.literal: When it came, it was usually at night, shining like a meteor, coming from the east and gathering at the shrine city, like a male pheasant; its sound rumbled, like wild fowl crying at night.
translations.idiomatic: When it came, it was usually at night, shining like a meteor, coming from the east and gathering at the shrine city, like a male pheasant; its sound rumbled, like wild fowl crying at night.

sentences.zh: 以一牢祠之，名曰陳寶。

translations.literal: It was sacrificed to with one complete victim and named the Chen Treasure.
translations.idiomatic: It was sacrificed to with one complete victim and named the Chen Treasure.

sentences.zh: 作陳寶祠後七十一年，秦德公立，卜居雍。

translations.literal: Seventy-one years after establishing the Chen Treasure shrine, Duke De of Qin took the throne and divined to settle at Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Seventy-one years after establishing the Chen Treasure shrine, Duke De of Qin took the throne and divined to settle at Yong.

sentences.zh: 子孫飲馬於河，遂都雍。

translations.literal: His descendants watered horses at the river and then made Yong the capital.
translations.idiomatic: His descendants watered horses at the river and then made Yong the capital.

sentences.zh: 雍之諸祠自此興。

translations.literal: From this point, the shrines at Yong flourished.
translations.idiomatic: From this point, the shrines at Yong flourished.

sentences.zh: 用三百牢於鄜畤。

translations.literal: Three hundred complete sacrificial victims were used at the Fu altar.
translations.idiomatic: Three hundred complete sacrificial victims were used at the Fu altar.

sentences.zh: 作伏祠。

translations.literal: He established the Fu sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: He established the Fu sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 磔狗邑四門，以御蠱災。

translations.literal: Dogs were dismembered at the four gates of the city to ward off bewitching calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Dogs were dismembered at the four gates of the city to ward off bewitching calamities.

sentences.zh: 後四年，秦宣公作密畤於渭南，祭青帝。

translations.literal: Four years later, Duke Xuan of Qin built the Mi altar south of the Wei to sacrifice to the Azure Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later, Duke Xuan of Qin built the Mi altar south of the Wei to sacrifice to the Azure Thearch.

sentences.zh: 後十三年，秦穆公立，病臥五日不寤；

translations.literal: Thirteen years later, Duke Mu of Qin took the throne and lay ill for five days without waking.
translations.idiomatic: Thirteen years later, Duke Mu of Qin took the throne and lay ill for five days without waking.

sentences.zh: 寤，乃言夢見上帝，上帝命穆公平晉亂。

translations.literal: When he awoke, he said he had dreamed of the High God, who commanded him to pacify turmoil in Jin.
translations.idiomatic: When he awoke, he said he had dreamed of the High God, who commanded him to pacify turmoil in Jin.

sentences.zh: 史書而藏之府。

translations.literal: The historian wrote this and stored it in the archives.
translations.idiomatic: The historian wrote this and stored it in the archives.

sentences.zh: 而後世皆曰上天。

translations.literal: Later generations all referred to this as Heaven Above.
translations.idiomatic: Later generations all referred to this as Heaven Above.

sentences.zh: 穆公立九年，齊桓公既霸，會諸侯於葵丘，而欲封禪。

translations.literal: In the ninth year of Duke Mu's reign, after Duke Huan of Qi had become hegemon and assembled the lords at Kuiqiu, he wanted to perform Feng and Shan rites.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year of Duke Mu's reign, after Duke Huan of Qi had become hegemon and assembled the lords at Kuiqiu, he wanted to perform Feng and Shan rites.

sentences.zh: 管仲曰：「古者封泰山禪梁父者七十二家，而夷吾所記者十有二焉。

translations.literal: Guan Zhong said, 'In antiquity there were seventy-two houses that performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Liangfu, and of these I, Yiwu, have records for twelve.
translations.idiomatic: Guan Zhong said, 'In antiquity there were seventy-two houses that performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Liangfu, and of these I, Yiwu, have records for twelve.

sentences.zh: 昔無懷氏封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: In the past, the Wuhuai clan performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: In the past, the Wuhuai clan performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 虙羲封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Fuxi performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Fuxi performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 神農氏封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: The Shennong clan performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: The Shennong clan performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 炎帝封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: The Flame Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: The Flame Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 黃帝封泰山，禪亭亭；

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Tingting;
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Tingting;

sentences.zh: 顓頊封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Zhuanxu performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Zhuanxu performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 帝嚳封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Emperor Ku performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ku performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 堯封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Yao performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Yao performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 舜封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Shun performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Shun performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 禹封泰山，禪會稽；

translations.literal: Yu performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Kuaiji;
translations.idiomatic: Yu performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Kuaiji;

sentences.zh: 湯封泰山，禪云云；

translations.literal: Tang performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;
translations.idiomatic: Tang performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Yunyun;

sentences.zh: 周成王封泰山，禪於社首：皆受命然後得封禪。」

translations.literal: King Cheng of Zhou performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Sheshou: all first received Heaven's mandate and only then could perform Feng and Shan.'
translations.idiomatic: King Cheng of Zhou performed Feng at Mount Tai and Shan at Sheshou: all first received Heaven's mandate and only then could perform Feng and Shan.'

sentences.zh: 桓公曰：「寡人北伐山戎，過孤竹；

translations.literal: Duke Huan said, 'I campaigned north against the Mountain Rong and passed through Guzhu;
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan said, 'I campaigned north against the Mountain Rong and passed through Guzhu;

sentences.zh: 西伐，束馬縣車，上卑耳之山；

translations.literal: I campaigned west, bound up the horses and hauled the chariots by hand, and climbed low Er Mountain;
translations.idiomatic: I campaigned west, bound up the horses and hauled the chariots by hand, and climbed low Er Mountain;

sentences.zh: 南伐至召陵，登熊耳山，以望江漢。

translations.literal: then campaigned south to Zhaoling, climbed Xionger Mountain, and looked over the Yangzi and Han rivers.
translations.idiomatic: then campaigned south to Zhaoling, climbed Xionger Mountain, and looked over the Yangzi and Han rivers.

sentences.zh: 兵車之會三，乘車之會六，九合諸侯，一匡天下，諸侯莫違我。

translations.literal: There were three assemblies of war chariots and six assemblies of riding chariots; I convened the feudal lords nine times and corrected the realm once, and no lord defied me.
translations.idiomatic: There were three assemblies of war chariots and six assemblies of riding chariots; I convened the feudal lords nine times and corrected the realm once, and no lord defied me.

sentences.zh: 昔三代受命，亦何以異乎？」

translations.literal: How then was this different from the three dynasties when they received Heaven's mandate?'
translations.idiomatic: How then was this different from the three dynasties when they received Heaven's mandate?'

sentences.zh: 於是管仲睹桓公不可窮以辭，因設之以事，曰：「古之封禪，鄗上黍，北里禾，所以為盛；

translations.literal: Guan Zhong saw Duke Huan could not be stopped by argument alone, so he redirected him through concrete requirements: 'In ancient Feng and Shan rites, glutinous millet from Hao and grain from Beili were used, as marks of abundance;
translations.idiomatic: Guan Zhong saw Duke Huan could not be stopped by argument alone, so he redirected him through concrete requirements: 'In ancient Feng and Shan rites, glutinous millet from Hao and grain from Beili were used, as marks of abundance;

sentences.zh: 江淮間一茅三脊，所以為藉也。

translations.literal: and triple-ribbed thatch from between the Yangzi and Huai was used for mats.
translations.idiomatic: and triple-ribbed thatch from between the Yangzi and Huai was used for mats.

sentences.zh: 東海致比目之魚，西海致比翼之鳥。

translations.literal: The Eastern Sea sent paired-eye fish; the Western Sea sent paired-wing birds.
translations.idiomatic: The Eastern Sea sent paired-eye fish; the Western Sea sent paired-wing birds.

sentences.zh: 然後物有不召而自至者十有五焉。

translations.literal: Only then would fifteen kinds of things arrive on their own without being summoned.
translations.idiomatic: Only then would fifteen kinds of things arrive on their own without being summoned.

sentences.zh: 今鳳皇麒麟不至，嘉禾不生，而蓬蒿藜莠茂，鴟梟群翔，而欲封禪，無乃不可乎？」

translations.literal: Now phoenixes and qilin do not appear, fine grain does not grow, while weeds flourish and owls swarm in flight - and you still wish to perform Feng and Shan; is that not impossible?'
translations.idiomatic: Now phoenixes and qilin do not appear, fine grain does not grow, while weeds flourish and owls swarm in flight - and you still wish to perform Feng and Shan; is that not impossible?'

sentences.zh: 於是桓公乃止。

translations.literal: So Duke Huan stopped.
translations.idiomatic: So Duke Huan stopped.

sentences.zh: 是歲，秦穆公納晉君夷吾。

translations.literal: That year, Duke Mu of Qin accepted Yiwu, ruler of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: That year, Duke Mu of Qin accepted Yiwu, ruler of Jin.

sentences.zh: 其後三置晉國之君，平其亂。

translations.literal: Later, he installed rulers of Jin three times and pacified its disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Later, he installed rulers of Jin three times and pacified its disorder.

sentences.zh: 穆公立三十九年而卒。

translations.literal: Duke Mu reigned thirty-nine years and died.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Mu reigned thirty-nine years and died.

sentences.zh: 是時，季氏專魯，旅於泰山，仲尼譏之。

translations.literal: At this time the Ji clan monopolized Lu and performed a mountain sacrifice at Mount Tai; Confucius criticized this.
translations.idiomatic: At this time the Ji clan monopolized Lu and performed a mountain sacrifice at Mount Tai; Confucius criticized this.

sentences.zh: 自秦宣公作密畤後二百五十年，而秦靈公於吳陽作上畤，祭黃帝；

translations.literal: Two hundred fifty years after Duke Xuan of Qin established the Mi altar, Duke Ling of Qin built the Upper altar at Wuyang to sacrifice to the Yellow Thearch,
translations.idiomatic: Two hundred fifty years after Duke Xuan of Qin established the Mi altar, Duke Ling of Qin built the Upper altar at Wuyang to sacrifice to the Yellow Thearch,

sentences.zh: 作下畤，祭炎帝。

translations.literal: and built the Lower altar to sacrifice to the Flame Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: and built the Lower altar to sacrifice to the Flame Thearch.

sentences.zh: 後四十八年，周太史儋見秦獻公曰：「周始與秦國合而別，別五百載當復合，合七十年而伯王出焉。」

translations.literal: Forty-eight years later, the Zhou Grand Historian Dan met Duke Xian of Qin and said: 'Zhou and Qin began united and then split; after five hundred years apart they will unite again, and seventy years after reunion a hegemon-king will arise.'
translations.idiomatic: Forty-eight years later, the Zhou Grand Historian Dan met Duke Xian of Qin and said: 'Zhou and Qin began united and then split; after five hundred years apart they will unite again, and seventy years after reunion a hegemon-king will arise.'

sentences.zh: 儋見後七年，櫟陽雨金，獻公自以為得金瑞，故作畦畤櫟陽，而祀白帝。

translations.literal: Seven years after Dan's audience, gold rained at Liyang. Duke Xian took this as an auspicious gold portent, so he built a border altar at Liyang and sacrificed to the White Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: Seven years after Dan's audience, gold rained at Liyang. Duke Xian took this as an auspicious gold portent, so he built a border altar at Liyang and sacrificed to the White Thearch.

sentences.zh: 後百一十歲，周赧王卒，九鼎入於秦。

translations.literal: One hundred ten years later, King Nan of Zhou died, and the Nine Tripods entered Qin.
translations.idiomatic: One hundred ten years later, King Nan of Zhou died, and the Nine Tripods entered Qin.

sentences.zh: 或曰，周顯王之四十二年，宋大丘社亡，而鼎淪沒於泗水彭城下。

translations.literal: Some say in the forty-second year of King Xian of Zhou, the altar of Daqiu in Song disappeared and the tripods sank beneath Pengcheng on the Si River.
translations.idiomatic: Some say in the forty-second year of King Xian of Zhou, the altar of Daqiu in Song disappeared and the tripods sank beneath Pengcheng on the Si River.

sentences.zh: 自赧王卒後七年，秦莊襄王滅東周，周祀絕。

translations.literal: Seven years after King Nan's death, King Zhuangxiang of Qin destroyed Eastern Zhou, and Zhou sacrifices were cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Seven years after King Nan's death, King Zhuangxiang of Qin destroyed Eastern Zhou, and Zhou sacrifices were cut off.

sentences.zh: 後二十八年，秦并天下，稱皇帝。

translations.literal: Twenty-eight years later, Qin unified the realm and took the title Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Twenty-eight years later, Qin unified the realm and took the title Emperor.

sentences.zh: 臘，獲黑龍，此其水德之瑞。」

translations.literal: At the year-end sacrifice, a black dragon was obtained; this was taken as the omen of Water Virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: At the year-end sacrifice, a black dragon was obtained; this was taken as the omen of Water Virtue.'

sentences.zh: 於是秦更名河曰「德水」，以冬十月為年首，色尚黑，度以六為名，音上大呂，事統上法。

translations.literal: Accordingly Qin renamed the Yellow River 'Virtue Water,' made the tenth month of winter the start of the year, revered black as the color, took six as its governing number, exalted the pitch Da Lu, and modeled affairs on legal standards.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly Qin renamed the Yellow River 'Virtue Water,' made the tenth month of winter the start of the year, revered black as the color, took six as its governing number, exalted the pitch Da Lu, and modeled affairs on legal standards.

sentences.zh: 即帝位三年，東巡狩郡縣，祠騶嶧山，頌功業。

translations.literal: In the third year after taking imperial rank, he made an eastern inspection of commanderies and counties, sacrificed at Zou and Yi mountains, and proclaimed his achievements.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year after taking imperial rank, he made an eastern inspection of commanderies and counties, sacrificed at Zou and Yi mountains, and proclaimed his achievements.

sentences.zh: 於是從齊魯之儒生博士七十人，至於泰山下。

translations.literal: He then brought along seventy Confucian erudites from Qi and Lu to the foot of Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: He then brought along seventy Confucian erudites from Qi and Lu to the foot of Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 諸儒生或議曰：「古者封禪為蒲車，惡傷山之土石草木；

translations.literal: Some of the scholars argued: 'In antiquity, for Feng and Shan one used reed carriages, so as not to injure the mountain's soil, stones, grasses, or trees;
translations.idiomatic: Some of the scholars argued: 'In antiquity, for Feng and Shan one used reed carriages, so as not to injure the mountain's soil, stones, grasses, or trees;

sentences.zh: 掃地而祠，席用苴峵，言其易遵也。」

translations.literal: one swept the ground and sacrificed, and mats were of coarse grass, showing the rite could be followed simply.'
translations.idiomatic: one swept the ground and sacrificed, and mats were of coarse grass, showing the rite could be followed simply.'

sentences.zh: 始皇聞此議各乖異，難施用，由此黜儒生。

translations.literal: When the First Emperor heard these proposals all diverged and were hard to implement, he dismissed the scholars.
translations.idiomatic: When the First Emperor heard these proposals all diverged and were hard to implement, he dismissed the scholars.

sentences.zh: 而遂除車道，上自泰山陽。

translations.literal: He then cleared a carriage road and ascended from the sunny side of Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: He then cleared a carriage road and ascended from the sunny side of Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 至顛，立石頌德，明其得封也。

translations.literal: At the summit he erected a stone to praise his virtue and make clear he had obtained the right to perform Feng.
translations.idiomatic: At the summit he erected a stone to praise his virtue and make clear he had obtained the right to perform Feng.

sentences.zh: 從陰道下，禪於梁父。

translations.literal: He descended by the shaded route and performed Shan at Liangfu.
translations.idiomatic: He descended by the shaded route and performed Shan at Liangfu.

sentences.zh: 其禮頗采泰祝之祀雍上帝所用，而封臧皆祕之，世不得而記也。

translations.literal: The ritual took some elements from the Yong suburban rites to the High Thearch as conducted by Qin invocators, but both Feng and hiding rites were kept secret, so later ages could not record them.
translations.idiomatic: The ritual took some elements from the Yong suburban rites to the High Thearch as conducted by Qin invocators, but both Feng and hiding rites were kept secret, so later ages could not record them.

sentences.zh: 始皇之上泰山，中阪遇暴風雨，休於大樹下。

translations.literal: When the First Emperor ascended Mount Tai, a violent storm struck him midway up; he rested under a great tree.
translations.idiomatic: When the First Emperor ascended Mount Tai, a violent storm struck him midway up; he rested under a great tree.

sentences.zh: 諸儒既黜，不得與封禪，聞始皇遇風雨，即譏之。

translations.literal: The scholars, having been dismissed and barred from participating, heard of the storm and mocked him.
translations.idiomatic: The scholars, having been dismissed and barred from participating, heard of the storm and mocked him.

sentences.zh: 於是始皇遂東遊海上，行禮祠名山川及八神，來僊人羨門之屬。

translations.literal: The First Emperor then traveled east along the sea, performing rites to famous mountains and rivers and to the Eight Spirits, and summoning transcendents such as Xianmen.
translations.idiomatic: The First Emperor then traveled east along the sea, performing rites to famous mountains and rivers and to the Eight Spirits, and summoning transcendents such as Xianmen.

sentences.zh: 八神將自古而有之；

translations.literal: The Eight Spirits were said to have existed since antiquity;
translations.idiomatic: The Eight Spirits were said to have existed since antiquity;

sentences.zh: 或曰太公以來作之。

translations.literal: some said they were instituted beginning with Taigong.
translations.idiomatic: some said they were instituted beginning with Taigong.

sentences.zh: 齊所以為齊，以天齊也。

translations.literal: Qi was called Qi because of Tianqi.
translations.idiomatic: Qi was called Qi because of Tianqi.

sentences.zh: 其祀絕，莫知起時。

translations.literal: Its sacrifice had lapsed, and no one knew when it began.
translations.idiomatic: Its sacrifice had lapsed, and no one knew when it began.

sentences.zh: 八神，一曰天主，祠天齊。

translations.literal: Of the Eight Spirits, first was the Heavenly Lord, worshiped at Tianqi.
translations.idiomatic: Of the Eight Spirits, first was the Heavenly Lord, worshiped at Tianqi.

sentences.zh: 天齊淵水，居臨菑南郊山下下者。

translations.literal: Tianqi was deep water at the foot of a low hill in the southern suburb of Linzi.
translations.idiomatic: Tianqi was deep water at the foot of a low hill in the southern suburb of Linzi.

sentences.zh: 二曰地主，祠泰山梁父。

translations.literal: Second was the Earthly Lord, worshiped at Liangfu of Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: Second was the Earthly Lord, worshiped at Liangfu of Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 蓋天好陰，祠之必於高山之下畤，命曰「畤」；

translations.literal: Heaven favors yin, so sacrifice to it must be at a low altar beneath a high mountain, called a zhi altar;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven favors yin, so sacrifice to it must be at a low altar beneath a high mountain, called a zhi altar;

sentences.zh: 地貴陽，祭之必於澤中圜丘云。

translations.literal: Earth honors yang, so sacrifice to it must be on a round mound in the midst of marshland.
translations.idiomatic: Earth honors yang, so sacrifice to it must be on a round mound in the midst of marshland.

sentences.zh: 三曰兵主，祠蚩尤。

translations.literal: Third was the Lord of War, worshiping Chiyou.
translations.idiomatic: Third was the Lord of War, worshiping Chiyou.

sentences.zh: 蚩尤在東平陸監鄉，齊之西竟也。

translations.literal: Chiyou's site was at Jian township in Dongpinglu, on Qi's western border.
translations.idiomatic: Chiyou's site was at Jian township in Dongpinglu, on Qi's western border.

sentences.zh: 四曰陰主，祠三山；

translations.literal: Fourth was the Yin Lord, worshiped at the Three Mountains;
translations.idiomatic: Fourth was the Yin Lord, worshiped at the Three Mountains;

sentences.zh: 五曰陽主，祠之罘山；

translations.literal: fifth was the Yang Lord, worshiped at Zhifu Mountain;
translations.idiomatic: fifth was the Yang Lord, worshiped at Zhifu Mountain;

sentences.zh: 六曰月主，祠之萊山：皆在齊北，並勃海。

translations.literal: sixth was the Moon Lord, worshiped at Lai Mountain: all were north of Qi along the Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: sixth was the Moon Lord, worshiped at Lai Mountain: all were north of Qi along the Bohai.

sentences.zh: 七曰日主，祠盛山。

translations.literal: Seventh was the Sun Lord, worshiped at Sheng Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Seventh was the Sun Lord, worshiped at Sheng Mountain.

sentences.zh: 盛山斗入海，最居齊東北陽，以迎日出云。

translations.literal: Sheng Mountain juts into the sea; it lies farthest to Qi's northeastern sunny side and was used to greet sunrise.
translations.idiomatic: Sheng Mountain juts into the sea; it lies farthest to Qi's northeastern sunny side and was used to greet sunrise.

sentences.zh: 八曰四時主，祠琅邪。

translations.literal: Eighth was the Lord of the Four Seasons, worshiped at Langya.
translations.idiomatic: Eighth was the Lord of the Four Seasons, worshiped at Langya.

sentences.zh: 琅邪在齊東北，蓋歲之所始。

translations.literal: Langya was in Qi's northeast, likely where the year's cycle began.
translations.idiomatic: Langya was in Qi's northeast, likely where the year's cycle began.

sentences.zh: 皆各用牢具祠，而巫祝所損益，圭幣雜異焉。

translations.literal: All were sacrificed to with full victim sets, though shamans and invocators adjusted details, and jade tablets and silk offerings varied.
translations.idiomatic: All were sacrificed to with full victim sets, though shamans and invocators adjusted details, and jade tablets and silk offerings varied.

sentences.zh: 自齊威、宣時，騶子之徒論著終始五德之運，及秦帝而齊人奏之，故始皇采用之。

translations.literal: From the time of Kings Wei and Xuan of Qi, disciples of Master Zou wrote on the cyclical movement of the Five Virtues; when Qin became imperial, Qi men presented these theories, so the First Emperor adopted them.
translations.idiomatic: From the time of Kings Wei and Xuan of Qi, disciples of Master Zou wrote on the cyclical movement of the Five Virtues; when Qin became imperial, Qi men presented these theories, so the First Emperor adopted them.

sentences.zh: 而宋毋忌、正伯僑、元尚、羨門高最後，皆燕人，為方僊道，形解銷化，依於鬼神之事。

translations.literal: Song Wuji, Zheng Boqiao, Yuan Shang, and lastly Xianmen Gao were all men of Yan; they practiced methods of transcendent arts, bodily dissolution and transformation, relying on dealings with spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Song Wuji, Zheng Boqiao, Yuan Shang, and lastly Xianmen Gao were all men of Yan; they practiced methods of transcendent arts, bodily dissolution and transformation, relying on dealings with spirits.

sentences.zh: 騶衍以陰陽主運顯於諸侯，而燕齊海上之方士傳其術不能通，然則怪迂阿諛苟合之徒自此興，不可勝數也。

translations.literal: Zou Yan became prominent among lords through yin-yang and cyclical governance, while fangshi along the Yan-Qi coast transmitted his arts without mastering them. From this point, strange and devious flatterers who sought opportunistic alignment arose in numbers beyond count.
translations.idiomatic: Zou Yan became prominent among lords through yin-yang and cyclical governance, while fangshi along the Yan-Qi coast transmitted his arts without mastering them. From this point, strange and devious flatterers who sought opportunistic alignment arose in numbers beyond count.

sentences.zh: 自威、宣、燕昭使人入海求蓬萊、方丈、瀛洲。

translations.literal: From the reigns of Wei and Xuan, and of King Zhao of Yan, men were sent to sea to seek Penglai, Fangzhang, and Yingzhou.
translations.idiomatic: From the reigns of Wei and Xuan, and of King Zhao of Yan, men were sent to sea to seek Penglai, Fangzhang, and Yingzhou.

sentences.zh: 此三神山者，其傳在勃海中，去人不遠。

translations.literal: These three spirit mountains were said to lie in the Bohai and not be far from people.
translations.idiomatic: These three spirit mountains were said to lie in the Bohai and not be far from people.

sentences.zh: 蓋嘗有至者，諸僊人及不死之藥皆在焉。

translations.literal: It was said some had reached them, and there all transcendents and the medicine of immortality were found.
translations.idiomatic: It was said some had reached them, and there all transcendents and the medicine of immortality were found.

sentences.zh: 其物禽獸盡白，而黃金銀為宮闕。

translations.literal: Their creatures were all white, and their palaces were of gold and silver.
translations.idiomatic: Their creatures were all white, and their palaces were of gold and silver.

sentences.zh: 未至，望之如雲；

translations.literal: Before arrival, they looked like clouds;
translations.idiomatic: Before arrival, they looked like clouds;

sentences.zh: 及到，三神山反居水下，水臨之。

translations.literal: upon approach, the three spirit mountains instead seemed beneath the water, with the water pressing over them.
translations.idiomatic: upon approach, the three spirit mountains instead seemed beneath the water, with the water pressing over them.

sentences.zh: 患且至，則風輒引船而去，終莫能至云。

translations.literal: And when ships were about to reach them, winds would suddenly drive the vessels away, so no one could ever arrive.
translations.idiomatic: And when ships were about to reach them, winds would suddenly drive the vessels away, so no one could ever arrive.

sentences.zh: 世主莫不甘心焉。

translations.literal: Yet rulers of the age all longed for them.
translations.idiomatic: Yet rulers of the age all longed for them.

sentences.zh: 及秦始皇至海上，則方士爭言之。

translations.literal: When Qin Shihuang reached the seacoast, fangshi competed in speaking of these matters.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin Shihuang reached the seacoast, fangshi competed in speaking of these matters.

sentences.zh: 始皇如恐弗及，使人齎童男女入海求之。

translations.literal: Fearing he might miss the chance, the First Emperor sent people carrying boys and girls out to sea to seek them.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing he might miss the chance, the First Emperor sent people carrying boys and girls out to sea to seek them.

sentences.zh: 船交海中，皆以風為解，曰未能至，望見之焉。

translations.literal: When ships crossed the sea, they all blamed winds and explained that they had not reached the islands, only seen them from afar.
translations.idiomatic: When ships crossed the sea, they all blamed winds and explained that they had not reached the islands, only seen them from afar.

sentences.zh: 其明年，始皇復游海上，至琅邪，過恆山，從上黨歸。

translations.literal: The following year, the First Emperor again toured the coast, reached Langya, passed Mount Heng, and returned via Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, the First Emperor again toured the coast, reached Langya, passed Mount Heng, and returned via Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 後三年，游碣石，考入海方士，從上郡歸。

translations.literal: Three years later, he toured Jieshi, examined fangshi who entered the sea, and returned via Shang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, he toured Jieshi, examined fangshi who entered the sea, and returned via Shang commandery.

sentences.zh: 後五年，始皇南至湘山，遂登會稽，並海上，幾遇海中三神山之奇藥。

translations.literal: Five years later, he went south to Xiang Mountain, then climbed Kuaiji and traveled along the sea, nearly encountering miraculous medicines from the three spirit mountains.
translations.idiomatic: Five years later, he went south to Xiang Mountain, then climbed Kuaiji and traveled along the sea, nearly encountering miraculous medicines from the three spirit mountains.

sentences.zh: 不得，還到沙丘崩。

translations.literal: He did not obtain them; on returning he collapsed and died at Shaqiu.
translations.idiomatic: He did not obtain them; on returning he collapsed and died at Shaqiu.

sentences.zh: 始皇封禪之後十二年而秦亡。

translations.literal: Twelve years after the First Emperor's Feng and Shan rites, Qin fell.
translations.idiomatic: Twelve years after the First Emperor's Feng and Shan rites, Qin fell.

sentences.zh: 諸儒生疾秦焚詩書，誅滅文學，百姓怨其法，天下叛之，皆說曰：「始皇上泰山，為風雨所擊，不得封禪云。」

translations.literal: Confucian scholars hated Qin for burning the Odes and Documents and executing scholars; the people resented Qin laws and rebelled across the realm. So all said: 'When the First Emperor climbed Mount Tai, he was struck by wind and rain and could not truly complete Feng and Shan.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucian scholars hated Qin for burning the Odes and Documents and executing scholars; the people resented Qin laws and rebelled across the realm. So all said: 'When the First Emperor climbed Mount Tai, he was struck by wind and rain and could not truly complete Feng and Shan.'

sentences.zh: 此豈所謂無其德而用其事者邪？

translations.literal: Is this not what is meant by using the rite without possessing the virtue for it?
translations.idiomatic: Is this not what is meant by using the rite without possessing the virtue for it?

sentences.zh: 昔三代之居皆河洛之間，故嵩高為中嶽，而四嶽各如其方，四瀆咸在山東。

translations.literal: In former times the three dynasties all dwelt between the Yellow and Luo rivers, so Songgao was the Central Peak, each of the four peaks matched its direction, and the four waterways all lay east of the mountains.
translations.idiomatic: In former times the three dynasties all dwelt between the Yellow and Luo rivers, so Songgao was the Central Peak, each of the four peaks matched its direction, and the four waterways all lay east of the mountains.

sentences.zh: 至秦稱帝，都咸陽，則五嶽、四瀆皆并在東方。

translations.literal: By Qin's imperial era, with the capital at Xianyang, the five peaks and four waterways were all grouped in the east.
translations.idiomatic: By Qin's imperial era, with the capital at Xianyang, the five peaks and four waterways were all grouped in the east.

sentences.zh: 自五帝以至秦，迭興迭衰，名山大川或在諸侯，或在天子，其禮損益世殊，不可勝記。

translations.literal: From the Five Thearchs to Qin, dynasties rose and fell in turn; famous mountains and great rivers were at times under feudal lords and at times under the Son of Heaven. Ritual additions and reductions differed by age and cannot be fully recorded.
translations.idiomatic: From the Five Thearchs to Qin, dynasties rose and fell in turn; famous mountains and great rivers were at times under feudal lords and at times under the Son of Heaven. Ritual additions and reductions differed by age and cannot be fully recorded.

sentences.zh: 及秦并天下，令祠官所常奉天地名山大川鬼神可得而序也。

translations.literal: After Qin unified the realm, however, the sacrificial offices could at last order in sequence those heavens, famous mountains, great rivers, and spirits they regularly served.
translations.idiomatic: After Qin unified the realm, however, the sacrificial offices could at last order in sequence those heavens, famous mountains, great rivers, and spirits they regularly served.

sentences.zh: 於是自崤以東，名山五，大川祠二。

translations.literal: Thus east of Xiao Pass there were five famous mountain shrines and two great river shrines.
translations.idiomatic: Thus east of Xiao Pass there were five famous mountain shrines and two great river shrines.

sentences.zh: 曰太室。

translations.literal: One was Taishi.
translations.idiomatic: One was Taishi.

sentences.zh: 太室，嵩高也。

translations.literal: Taishi is Songgao.
translations.idiomatic: Taishi is Songgao.

sentences.zh: 恆山，泰山，會稽，湘山。

translations.literal: Also Heng Mountain, Mount Tai, Kuaiji, and Xiang Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Also Heng Mountain, Mount Tai, Kuaiji, and Xiang Mountain.

sentences.zh: 水曰泲，曰淮。

translations.literal: The rivers were the Ji and the Huai.
translations.idiomatic: The rivers were the Ji and the Huai.

sentences.zh: 春以脯酒為歲禱，因泮凍；

translations.literal: In spring they offered dried meat and wine for annual prayers, timed with river thaw.
translations.idiomatic: In spring they offered dried meat and wine for annual prayers, timed with river thaw.

sentences.zh: 秋涸凍；

translations.literal: In autumn they prayed when waters ran dry and froze.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn they prayed when waters ran dry and froze.

sentences.zh: 冬塞禱祠。

translations.literal: In winter they performed closing prayers and sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: In winter they performed closing prayers and sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 其牲用牛犢各一，牢具圭幣各異。

translations.literal: Victims were one calf each, with full sacrificial sets; jade and silk offerings varied by shrine.
translations.idiomatic: Victims were one calf each, with full sacrificial sets; jade and silk offerings varied by shrine.

sentences.zh: 自華以西，名山七，名川四。

translations.literal: West of Mount Hua there were seven famous mountains and four famous rivers.
translations.idiomatic: West of Mount Hua there were seven famous mountains and four famous rivers.

sentences.zh: 曰華山，薄山。

translations.literal: They were Mount Hua and Mount Bo.
translations.idiomatic: They were Mount Hua and Mount Bo.

sentences.zh: 薄山者，襄山也。

translations.literal: Mount Bo was Xiang Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Bo was Xiang Mountain.

sentences.zh: 岳山，岐山，吳山，鴻冢，瀆山。

translations.literal: Also Yue Mountain, Qi Mountain, Wu Mountain, Hongzhong, and Du Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Also Yue Mountain, Qi Mountain, Wu Mountain, Hongzhong, and Du Mountain.

sentences.zh: 瀆山，蜀之岷山也。

translations.literal: Du Mountain is Min Mountain in Shu.
translations.idiomatic: Du Mountain is Min Mountain in Shu.

sentences.zh: 水曰河，祠臨晉；

translations.literal: For waters: the Yellow River was sacrificed to at Linjin;
translations.idiomatic: For waters: the Yellow River was sacrificed to at Linjin;

sentences.zh: 沔，祠漢中；

translations.literal: the Mian at Hanzhong;
translations.idiomatic: the Mian at Hanzhong;

sentences.zh: 湫淵，祠朝那；

translations.literal: Qiuyuan at Chaona;
translations.idiomatic: Qiuyuan at Chaona;

sentences.zh: 江水，祠蜀。

translations.literal: and the Yangzi at Shu.
translations.idiomatic: and the Yangzi at Shu.

sentences.zh: 亦春秋泮涸禱塞如東方山川；

translations.literal: Spring and autumn thaw/dry prayers and winter closing rites were as with eastern mountains and rivers;
translations.idiomatic: Spring and autumn thaw/dry prayers and winter closing rites were as with eastern mountains and rivers;

sentences.zh: 而牲亦牛犢牢具圭幣各異。

translations.literal: and offerings likewise used calves, full sacrificial sets, and varied jade and silk by shrine.
translations.idiomatic: and offerings likewise used calves, full sacrificial sets, and varied jade and silk by shrine.

sentences.zh: 而四大冢鴻、岐、吳、嶽，皆有嘗禾。

translations.literal: And at the four great mounds - Hong, Qi, Wu, and Yue - there were also first-fruits offerings of grain.
translations.idiomatic: And at the four great mounds - Hong, Qi, Wu, and Yue - there were also first-fruits offerings of grain.

sentences.zh: 陳寶節來祠，其河加有嘗醪。

translations.literal: At seasonal visits to the Chen Treasure shrine, river rites also added first-tasting new sweet wine.
translations.idiomatic: At seasonal visits to the Chen Treasure shrine, river rites also added first-tasting new sweet wine.

sentences.zh: 此皆雍州之域，近天子都，故加車一乘，騮駒四。

translations.literal: All these lay within Yongzhou, near the Son of Heaven's capital, so one extra chariot and four red colts were added.
translations.idiomatic: All these lay within Yongzhou, near the Son of Heaven's capital, so one extra chariot and four red colts were added.

sentences.zh: 霸、產、豐、澇、涇、渭、長水，皆不在大山川數，以近咸陽，盡得比山川祠，而無諸加。

translations.literal: The Ba, Chan, Feng, Lao, Jing, Wei, and Chang rivers were not counted among major mountain-river shrines, but because they were near Xianyang they all received rites comparable to mountain-river sacrifice, though without additional extras.
translations.idiomatic: The Ba, Chan, Feng, Lao, Jing, Wei, and Chang rivers were not counted among major mountain-river shrines, but because they were near Xianyang they all received rites comparable to mountain-river sacrifice, though without additional extras.

sentences.zh: 汧、洛二淵，鳴澤、蒲山、嶽婿山之屬，為小山川，亦皆禱塞泮涸祠，禮不必同。

translations.literal: The Qian and Luo pools, Ming Marsh, Pu Mountain, Yuexu Mountain and the like were treated as minor mountain-river shrines, also with thaw, dry, and closing prayers, though their rites were not necessarily identical.
translations.idiomatic: The Qian and Luo pools, Ming Marsh, Pu Mountain, Yuexu Mountain and the like were treated as minor mountain-river shrines, also with thaw, dry, and closing prayers, though their rites were not necessarily identical.

sentences.zh: 而雍有日、月、參、辰、南北斗、熒惑、太白、歲星、填星、辰星、二十八宿、風伯、雨師、四海、九臣、十四臣、諸布、諸嚴、諸逐之屬，百有餘廟。

translations.literal: At Yong there were also over a hundred temples for the sun, moon, Shen and Chen stars, northern and southern dippers, Mars, Venus, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, the twenty-eight lodges, Earl of Wind, Master of Rain, the Four Seas, Nine Ministers, Fourteen Ministers, and many other deities.
translations.idiomatic: At Yong there were also over a hundred temples for the sun, moon, Shen and Chen stars, northern and southern dippers, Mars, Venus, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, the twenty-eight lodges, Earl of Wind, Master of Rain, the Four Seas, Nine Ministers, Fourteen Ministers, and many other deities.

sentences.zh: 西亦有數十祠。

translations.literal: To the west there were likewise several dozen shrines.
translations.idiomatic: To the west there were likewise several dozen shrines.

sentences.zh: 於湖有周天子祠。

translations.literal: At Hu there was a shrine to the Zhou Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: At Hu there was a shrine to the Zhou Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 於下邽有天神。

translations.literal: At Xiagui there was a Heavenly Spirit shrine.
translations.idiomatic: At Xiagui there was a Heavenly Spirit shrine.

sentences.zh: 豐、鎬有昭明、天子辟池。

translations.literal: At Feng and Hao there were shrines to Zhaoming and the Son of Heaven's Bi Pool.
translations.idiomatic: At Feng and Hao there were shrines to Zhaoming and the Son of Heaven's Bi Pool.

sentences.zh: 於杜、亳有五杜主之祠、壽星祠；

translations.literal: At Du and Bo there were shrines to the Five Lords of Du and to the Longevity Star;
translations.idiomatic: At Du and Bo there were shrines to the Five Lords of Du and to the Longevity Star;

sentences.zh: 而雍、菅廟祠亦有杜主。

translations.literal: and at Yong and Jian temples there were also Du Lord shrines.
translations.idiomatic: and at Yong and Jian temples there were also Du Lord shrines.

sentences.zh: 杜主，故周之右將軍，其在秦中最小鬼之神者也。

translations.literal: The Du Lord was an old Right General of Zhou; in Guanzhong he was ranked among the smallest ghost-deities.
translations.idiomatic: The Du Lord was an old Right General of Zhou; in Guanzhong he was ranked among the smallest ghost-deities.

sentences.zh: 各以歲時奉祠。

translations.literal: Each received seasonal sacrifices according to the calendar.
translations.idiomatic: Each received seasonal sacrifices according to the calendar.

sentences.zh: 唯雍四時上帝為尊，其光景動人民，唯陳寶。

translations.literal: Among them, only the Four Seasonal High Thearch rites at Yong were supreme; among moving apparitions that affected the people, only the Chen Treasure counted.
translations.idiomatic: Among them, only the Four Seasonal High Thearch rites at Yong were supreme; among moving apparitions that affected the people, only the Chen Treasure counted.

sentences.zh: 故雍四畤，春以為歲祠禱，因泮凍，秋涸凍，冬賽祠，五月嘗駒，及四中之月月祠，若陳寶節來一祠。

translations.literal: Therefore at Yong's four altars, spring rites were annual prayers timed with thaw, autumn rites with drying and freeze, winter rites were closing sacrifices; in the fifth month there was a colt offering; in the middle month of each season there were monthly rites; and when the Chen Treasure's season arrived there was one sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore at Yong's four altars, spring rites were annual prayers timed with thaw, autumn rites with drying and freeze, winter rites were closing sacrifices; in the fifth month there was a colt offering; in the middle month of each season there were monthly rites; and when the Chen Treasure's season arrived there was one sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 春夏用騂，秋冬用騮。

translations.literal: Spring and summer used red victims; autumn and winter used red-brown ones.
translations.idiomatic: Spring and summer used red victims; autumn and winter used red-brown ones.

sentences.zh: 畤駒四匹，木寓龍一駟，木寓車馬一駟，各如其帝色。

translations.literal: At the altars were four colt victims, one quadriga of wooden symbolic dragons, and one quadriga of wooden symbolic chariot-horses, each matching the color of its Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: At the altars were four colt victims, one quadriga of wooden symbolic dragons, and one quadriga of wooden symbolic chariot-horses, each matching the color of its Thearch.

sentences.zh: 黃犢羔各四，圭幣各有數，皆生瘞埋，無俎豆之具。

translations.literal: There were four yellow calves and four lambs, with set numbers of jade and silk; all were buried alive, with no tripod-and-bowl serving implements.
translations.idiomatic: There were four yellow calves and four lambs, with set numbers of jade and silk; all were buried alive, with no tripod-and-bowl serving implements.

sentences.zh: 三年一郊。

translations.literal: A suburban sacrifice was held once every three years.
translations.idiomatic: A suburban sacrifice was held once every three years.

sentences.zh: 秦以十月為歲首，故常以十月上宿郊見，通權火，拜於咸陽之旁，而衣上白，其用如經祠云。

translations.literal: Qin took the tenth month as year start, so they regularly made predawn suburban audience rites in that month, passed the torch-fire, and bowed beside Xianyang, wearing upper white garments; usage followed the canonical sacrificial procedure.
translations.idiomatic: Qin took the tenth month as year start, so they regularly made predawn suburban audience rites in that month, passed the torch-fire, and bowed beside Xianyang, wearing upper white garments; usage followed the canonical sacrificial procedure.

sentences.zh: 西畤、畦畤，祠如其故，上不親往。

translations.literal: At the Western altar and border altar, rites followed old practice; the ruler did not go in person.
translations.idiomatic: At the Western altar and border altar, rites followed old practice; the ruler did not go in person.

sentences.zh: 諸此祠皆太祝常主，以歲時奉祠之。

translations.literal: All these sacrifices were routinely overseen by the Grand Invocator, who conducted them seasonally.
translations.idiomatic: All these sacrifices were routinely overseen by the Grand Invocator, who conducted them seasonally.

sentences.zh: 至如它名山川諸神及八神之屬，上過則祠，去則已。

translations.literal: As for other famous mountains, rivers, and spirit categories such as the Eight Spirits, the ruler sacrificed when passing by and stopped when he left.
translations.idiomatic: As for other famous mountains, rivers, and spirit categories such as the Eight Spirits, the ruler sacrificed when passing by and stopped when he left.

sentences.zh: 郡縣遠方祠者，民各自奉祠，不領於天子之祝官。

translations.literal: In distant commandery and county shrines, local people maintained rites themselves and were not administered by the Son of Heaven's invocator offices.
translations.idiomatic: In distant commandery and county shrines, local people maintained rites themselves and were not administered by the Son of Heaven's invocator offices.

sentences.zh: 祝官有祕祝，即有災祥，輒祝祠移過於下。

translations.literal: Invocator offices had secret rites: whenever omens of disaster or auspice appeared, they would transfer blame downward through ritual.
translations.idiomatic: Invocator offices had secret rites: whenever omens of disaster or auspice appeared, they would transfer blame downward through ritual.

sentences.zh: 漢興，高祖初起，殺大蛇，有物曰：「蛇，白帝子，而殺者赤帝子也。」

translations.literal: When Han first arose, Gaozu at the outset slew a great snake; a voice said, 'The snake was a son of the White Thearch, and the slayer is a son of the Red Thearch.'
translations.idiomatic: When Han first arose, Gaozu at the outset slew a great snake; a voice said, 'The snake was a son of the White Thearch, and the slayer is a son of the Red Thearch.'

sentences.zh: 及高祖禱豐枌榆社，侚沛，為沛公，則祀蚩尤，釁鼓旗。

translations.literal: When Gaozu prayed at the Fenyu Elm altar in Feng and patrolled Pei as Duke of Pei, he then sacrificed to Chiyou and consecrated drums and banners with blood.
translations.idiomatic: When Gaozu prayed at the Fenyu Elm altar in Feng and patrolled Pei as Duke of Pei, he then sacrificed to Chiyou and consecrated drums and banners with blood.

sentences.zh: 遂以十月至霸上，立為漢王。

translations.literal: He then reached Bashang in the tenth month and was established as King of Han.
translations.idiomatic: He then reached Bashang in the tenth month and was established as King of Han.

sentences.zh: 因以十月為年首，色上赤。

translations.literal: Accordingly he took the tenth month as the year's beginning and honored red as the state color.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly he took the tenth month as the year's beginning and honored red as the state color.

sentences.zh: 二年冬，東擊項籍而還入關，問：「故秦時上帝祠何帝也？」

translations.literal: In winter of the second year, after striking Xiang Ji in the east and returning within the passes, he asked: 'To which Thearch were Qin's High Thearch sacrifices made?'
translations.idiomatic: In winter of the second year, after striking Xiang Ji in the east and returning within the passes, he asked: 'To which Thearch were Qin's High Thearch sacrifices made?'

sentences.zh: 對曰：「四帝，有白、青、黃、赤帝之祠。」

translations.literal: They replied: 'There were four Thearch shrines - White, Azure, Yellow, and Red.'
translations.idiomatic: They replied: 'There were four Thearch shrines - White, Azure, Yellow, and Red.'

sentences.zh: 高祖曰：「吾聞天有五帝，而四，何也？」

translations.literal: Gaozu said: 'I have heard Heaven has Five Thearchs; why only four?'
translations.idiomatic: Gaozu said: 'I have heard Heaven has Five Thearchs; why only four?'

sentences.zh: 莫知其說。

translations.literal: No one could explain.
translations.idiomatic: No one could explain.

sentences.zh: 於是高祖曰：「吾知之矣，乃待我而具五也。」

translations.literal: So Gaozu said: 'I understand. The fifth awaits me to complete the set.'
translations.idiomatic: So Gaozu said: 'I understand. The fifth awaits me to complete the set.'

sentences.zh: 乃立黑帝祠，名曰北畤。

translations.literal: He then established a shrine to the Black Thearch, called the Northern altar.
translations.idiomatic: He then established a shrine to the Black Thearch, called the Northern altar.

sentences.zh: 有司進祠，上不親往。

translations.literal: Officials advanced the sacrifice; the emperor did not go personally.
translations.idiomatic: Officials advanced the sacrifice; the emperor did not go personally.

sentences.zh: 悉召故秦祀官，復置太祝、太宰，如其故儀禮。

translations.literal: He summoned all former Qin sacrificial officers and restored the offices of Grand Invocator and Grand Steward in their old ritual forms.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned all former Qin sacrificial officers and restored the offices of Grand Invocator and Grand Steward in their old ritual forms.

sentences.zh: 因令縣為公社。

translations.literal: He also ordered counties to establish public community altars.
translations.idiomatic: He also ordered counties to establish public community altars.

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：「吾甚重祠而敬祭。

translations.literal: He issued an edict: 'I place great weight on sacrifice and reverent offerings.
translations.idiomatic: He issued an edict: 'I place great weight on sacrifice and reverent offerings.

sentences.zh: 今上帝之祭及山川諸神當祠者，各以其時禮祠之如故。」

translations.literal: From now on, sacrifices due to the High Thearch and all mountain-river deities shall be performed in proper season and rite as before.'
translations.idiomatic: From now on, sacrifices due to the High Thearch and all mountain-river deities shall be performed in proper season and rite as before.'

sentences.zh: 後四歲，天下已定，詔御史令豐治枌榆社，常以時，春以羊彘祠之。

translations.literal: Four years later, with the realm settled, he ordered the Censor to manage the Fenyu altar in Feng, regularly sacrificing in season, with sheep and pigs in spring.
translations.idiomatic: Four years later, with the realm settled, he ordered the Censor to manage the Fenyu altar in Feng, regularly sacrificing in season, with sheep and pigs in spring.

sentences.zh: 令祝立蚩尤之祠於長安。

translations.literal: He ordered invocators to establish a Chiyou shrine in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered invocators to establish a Chiyou shrine in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 長安置祠祀官、女巫。

translations.literal: At Chang'an, sacrificial officers and female shamans were installed.
translations.idiomatic: At Chang'an, sacrificial officers and female shamans were installed.

sentences.zh: 其梁巫祠天、地、天社、天水、房中、當上之屬；

translations.literal: The Liang shamans sacrificed to Heaven, Earth, Heavenly Altars, Heavenly Waters, Chamber rites, and related upper deities.
translations.idiomatic: The Liang shamans sacrificed to Heaven, Earth, Heavenly Altars, Heavenly Waters, Chamber rites, and related upper deities.

sentences.zh: 晉巫祠五帝、東君、雲中君、巫社、巫祠、族人炊之屬；

translations.literal: The Jin shamans sacrificed to the Five Thearchs, Lord of the East, Lord in the Clouds, shaman community gods, shaman shrines, and clan-cooking gods.
translations.idiomatic: The Jin shamans sacrificed to the Five Thearchs, Lord of the East, Lord in the Clouds, shaman community gods, shaman shrines, and clan-cooking gods.

sentences.zh: 秦巫祠杜主、巫保、族纍之屬；

translations.literal: The Qin shamans sacrificed to the Du Lord, shaman protectors, and clan-linked deities.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin shamans sacrificed to the Du Lord, shaman protectors, and clan-linked deities.

sentences.zh: 荊巫祠堂下、巫先、司命、施糜之屬；

translations.literal: The Chu shamans sacrificed to Hall-Below, Former Shamans, Controller of Fate, and Shimi-type deities.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu shamans sacrificed to Hall-Below, Former Shamans, Controller of Fate, and Shimi-type deities.

sentences.zh: 九天巫祠九天：皆以歲時祠宮中。

translations.literal: Nine-Heavens shamans sacrificed to the Nine Heavens; all these rites were performed seasonally within the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Nine-Heavens shamans sacrificed to the Nine Heavens; all these rites were performed seasonally within the palace.

sentences.zh: 其河巫祠河於臨晉，而南山巫祠南山、秦中。

translations.literal: River shamans sacrificed to the River at Linjin, and Southern-Mountain shamans sacrificed to the Southern Mountain and to Qinzhong.
translations.idiomatic: River shamans sacrificed to the River at Linjin, and Southern-Mountain shamans sacrificed to the Southern Mountain and to Qinzhong.

sentences.zh: 秦中者，二世皇帝也。

translations.literal: Qinzhong referred to the Second Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Qinzhong referred to the Second Emperor.

sentences.zh: 各有時日。

translations.literal: Each rite had fixed times and days.
translations.idiomatic: Each rite had fixed times and days.

sentences.zh: 其後二歲，或言曰周興而邑立后稷之祠，至今血食天下。

translations.literal: Two years later, someone said: when Zhou rose and founded its capital, it established Houji's shrine, and to this day he receives blood sacrifice throughout the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, someone said: when Zhou rose and founded its capital, it established Houji's shrine, and to this day he receives blood sacrifice throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 於是高祖制詔御史：「其令天下立靈星祠，常以歲時祠以牛。」

translations.literal: So Gaozu decreed to the Censor: 'Order the whole realm to establish Spirit-Star shrines and regularly sacrifice to them by season with oxen.'
translations.idiomatic: So Gaozu decreed to the Censor: 'Order the whole realm to establish Spirit-Star shrines and regularly sacrifice to them by season with oxen.'

sentences.zh: 高祖十年春，有司請令縣常以春二月及臘祠稷以羊彘，民里社各自裁以祠。

translations.literal: In spring of Gaozu's tenth year, officials requested that counties regularly sacrifice to Millet in spring second month and at year-end with sheep and pigs, while local community altars should set their own scale for offerings.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of Gaozu's tenth year, officials requested that counties regularly sacrifice to Millet in spring second month and at year-end with sheep and pigs, while local community altars should set their own scale for offerings.

sentences.zh: 制曰：「可。」

translations.literal: The decree said: 'Approved.'
translations.idiomatic: The decree said: 'Approved.'

sentences.zh: 文帝即位十三年，下詔曰：「祕祝之官移過於下，朕甚弗取，其除之。」

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year of Emperor Wen's reign, he issued an edict: 'I strongly disapprove of the secret-invocator practice of shifting blame downward; abolish it.'
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year of Emperor Wen's reign, he issued an edict: 'I strongly disapprove of the secret-invocator practice of shifting blame downward; abolish it.'

sentences.zh: 始名山大川在諸侯，諸侯祝各自奉祠，天子官不領。

translations.literal: Originally, famous mountains and great rivers were under feudal lords, whose invocators each maintained rites independently, beyond imperial sacrificial offices.
translations.idiomatic: Originally, famous mountains and great rivers were under feudal lords, whose invocators each maintained rites independently, beyond imperial sacrificial offices.

sentences.zh: 及齊、淮南國廢，令太祝盡以歲時致禮如故。

translations.literal: After the states of Qi and Huainan were abolished, the Grand Invocator was ordered to deliver seasonal rites to them all as before.
translations.idiomatic: After the states of Qi and Huainan were abolished, the Grand Invocator was ordered to deliver seasonal rites to them all as before.

sentences.zh: 明年，以歲比登，詔有司增雍五畤路車各一乘，駕被具；

translations.literal: The following year, because harvests had been good, an edict ordered officials to add one road-chariot each with harness to the five Yong altars;
translations.idiomatic: The following year, because harvests had been good, an edict ordered officials to add one road-chariot each with harness to the five Yong altars;

sentences.zh: 西畤、畦畤寓車各一乘，寓馬四匹，駕被具；

translations.literal: and at the Western altar and border altar, to add one symbolic chariot and four symbolic horses each, with full harness.
translations.idiomatic: and at the Western altar and border altar, to add one symbolic chariot and four symbolic horses each, with full harness.

sentences.zh: 河、湫、漢水，玉加各二；

translations.literal: For the River, Qiu, and Han waters, two additional jade offerings were added to each;
translations.idiomatic: For the River, Qiu, and Han waters, two additional jade offerings were added to each;

sentences.zh: 及諸祀皆廣壇場，圭幣俎豆以差加之。

translations.literal: and for all other sacrifices, altar grounds were enlarged and jade, silk, tripods, and bowls were increased by grade.
translations.idiomatic: and for all other sacrifices, altar grounds were enlarged and jade, silk, tripods, and bowls were increased by grade.

sentences.zh: 魯人公孫臣上書曰：「始秦得水德，及漢受之，推終始傳，則漢當土德，土德之應黃龍見。

translations.literal: Gongsun Chen of Lu submitted a memorial: 'Qin first obtained Water Virtue. Han inherited it; if one follows the transmission cycle of beginning and end, Han should now be Earth Virtue, whose sign is the appearance of a yellow dragon.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Chen of Lu submitted a memorial: 'Qin first obtained Water Virtue. Han inherited it; if one follows the transmission cycle of beginning and end, Han should now be Earth Virtue, whose sign is the appearance of a yellow dragon.

sentences.zh: 宜改正朔，服色上黃。」

translations.literal: The calendar should be changed, and yellow should be honored in official dress.'
translations.idiomatic: The calendar should be changed, and yellow should be honored in official dress.'

sentences.zh: 時丞相張蒼好律曆，以為漢乃水德之時，河決金隄，其符也。

translations.literal: At the time Chancellor Zhang Cang favored calendrics and pitches and held that Han remained in Water Virtue; the Yellow River's breach of the Golden Dike was its confirming sign.
translations.idiomatic: At the time Chancellor Zhang Cang favored calendrics and pitches and held that Han remained in Water Virtue; the Yellow River's breach of the Golden Dike was its confirming sign.

sentences.zh: 年始冬十月，色外黑內赤，與德相應。

translations.literal: The year began in winter tenth month, with outer black and inner red colors, matching the virtue.
translations.idiomatic: The year began in winter tenth month, with outer black and inner red colors, matching the virtue.

sentences.zh: 公孫臣言非是，罷之。

translations.literal: He judged Gongsun Chen's claim incorrect and dismissed it.
translations.idiomatic: He judged Gongsun Chen's claim incorrect and dismissed it.

sentences.zh: 明年，黃龍見成紀。

translations.literal: The next year, a yellow dragon appeared at Chengji.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, a yellow dragon appeared at Chengji.

sentences.zh: 文帝召公孫臣，拜為博士，與諸生申明土德，草改曆服色事。

translations.literal: Emperor Wen summoned Gongsun Chen, appointed him Erudite, and with scholars restated Earth Virtue and drafted calendar and dress-color reforms.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen summoned Gongsun Chen, appointed him Erudite, and with scholars restated Earth Virtue and drafted calendar and dress-color reforms.

sentences.zh: 其夏，下詔曰：「有異物之神見於成紀，毋害於民，歲以有年。

translations.literal: That summer he issued an edict: 'A spirit of strange form has appeared at Chengji, harming no people, and the year has been fertile.
translations.idiomatic: That summer he issued an edict: 'A spirit of strange form has appeared at Chengji, harming no people, and the year has been fertile.

sentences.zh: 朕幾郊祀上帝諸神，禮官議，毋諱以朕勞。」

translations.literal: I am about to perform suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch and the gods. Let ritual officials deliberate, and do not avoid proposals out of concern for my labor.'
translations.idiomatic: I am about to perform suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch and the gods. Let ritual officials deliberate, and do not avoid proposals out of concern for my labor.'

sentences.zh: 有司皆曰：「古者天子夏親郊祀上帝於郊，故曰郊。」

translations.literal: All officials said: 'In antiquity the Son of Heaven in summer personally performed suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch; therefore it is called suburban sacrifice.'
translations.idiomatic: All officials said: 'In antiquity the Son of Heaven in summer personally performed suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch; therefore it is called suburban sacrifice.'

sentences.zh: 於是夏四月，文帝始幸雍郊見五畤，祠衣皆上赤。
