translations.literal: So in summer, fourth month, Emperor Wen first went in person to Yong and made suburban audience rites at the five altars, with sacrificial garments all honoring red.
translations.idiomatic: So in summer, fourth month, Emperor Wen first went in person to Yong and made suburban audience rites at the five altars, with sacrificial garments all honoring red.

sentences.zh: 趙人新垣平以望氣見上，言「長安東北有神氣，成五采，若人冠冕焉。

translations.literal: A man of Zhao, Xinyuan Ping, was presented after observing qi and said: 'Northeast of Chang'an there is spirit-vapor forming five colors, like a crowned figure.
translations.idiomatic: A man of Zhao, Xinyuan Ping, was presented after observing qi and said: 'Northeast of Chang'an there is spirit-vapor forming five colors, like a crowned figure.

sentences.zh: 或曰東北神明之舍，西方神明之墓也。

translations.literal: Some say the northeast is the dwelling of numinous powers, and the west their burial ground.
translations.idiomatic: Some say the northeast is the dwelling of numinous powers, and the west their burial ground.

sentences.zh: 天瑞下，宜立祠上帝，以合符應。」

translations.literal: Since Heavenly auspice has descended, a shrine to the High Thearch should be established to match the omen.'
translations.idiomatic: Since Heavenly auspice has descended, a shrine to the High Thearch should be established to match the omen.'

sentences.zh: 於是作渭陽五帝廟，同宇，帝一殿，面五門，各如其帝色。

translations.literal: Thus a Five-Thearch temple was built at Weiyang, sharing one compound: one hall per Thearch, facing five gates, each in its Thearch's color.
translations.idiomatic: Thus a Five-Thearch temple was built at Weiyang, sharing one compound: one hall per Thearch, facing five gates, each in its Thearch's color.

sentences.zh: 祠所用及儀亦如雍五畤。

translations.literal: Its sacrificial procedures and ritual forms followed those of Yong's five altars.
translations.idiomatic: Its sacrificial procedures and ritual forms followed those of Yong's five altars.

sentences.zh: 明年夏四月，文帝親拜霸渭之會，以郊見渭陽五帝。

translations.literal: In the next year, summer fourth month, Emperor Wen personally performed obeisance at the Ba-Wei confluence and made suburban audience rites to the Five Thearchs at Weiyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the next year, summer fourth month, Emperor Wen personally performed obeisance at the Ba-Wei confluence and made suburban audience rites to the Five Thearchs at Weiyang.

sentences.zh: 五帝廟臨渭，其北穿蒲池溝水。

translations.literal: The Five-Thearch temple stood by the Wei; to its north a pond-canal was dug to carry water.
translations.idiomatic: The Five-Thearch temple stood by the Wei; to its north a pond-canal was dug to carry water.

sentences.zh: 權火舉而祠，若光煇然屬天焉。

translations.literal: Torch-fire was raised for the rite, and light seemed to link upward to Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Torch-fire was raised for the rite, and light seemed to link upward to Heaven.

sentences.zh: 於是貴平至上大夫，賜累千金。

translations.literal: Thereupon Ping was elevated to Grand Master and granted several thousand in gold.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon Ping was elevated to Grand Master and granted several thousand in gold.

sentences.zh: 而使博士諸生刺六經中作王制，謀議巡狩封禪事。

translations.literal: The emperor also ordered Erudites and scholars to extract from the Six Classics a royal-regulation text and deliberate on inspection tours and Feng-Shan rites.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor also ordered Erudites and scholars to extract from the Six Classics a royal-regulation text and deliberate on inspection tours and Feng-Shan rites.

sentences.zh: 文帝出長門，若見五人於道北，遂因其直立五帝壇，祠以五牢。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wen went out through Changmen, he seemed to see five men north of the road, and so set up five Thearch altars on that line, sacrificing with five complete victims.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wen went out through Changmen, he seemed to see five men north of the road, and so set up five Thearch altars on that line, sacrificing with five complete victims.

sentences.zh: 其明年，平使人持玉杯，上書闕下獻之。

translations.literal: The following year, Ping sent someone carrying a jade cup to submit a memorial below the palace gate and present it.
translations.idiomatic: The following year, Ping sent someone carrying a jade cup to submit a memorial below the palace gate and present it.

sentences.zh: 平言上曰：「闕下有寶玉氣來者。」

translations.literal: Ping told the emperor: 'A precious jade aura has arrived beneath the palace gate.'
translations.idiomatic: Ping told the emperor: 'A precious jade aura has arrived beneath the palace gate.'

sentences.zh: 已視之，果有獻玉杯者，刻曰「人主延壽」。

translations.literal: When inspected, there truly was someone presenting a jade cup, inscribed: 'May the ruler prolong his life.'
translations.idiomatic: When inspected, there truly was someone presenting a jade cup, inscribed: 'May the ruler prolong his life.'

sentences.zh: 平又言「

translations.literal: Ping also said,
translations.idiomatic: Ping also said,

sentences.zh: 臣候日再中」。

translations.literal: 'Your servant predicts the sun will be at noon twice.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Your servant predicts the sun will be at noon twice.'

sentences.zh: 居頃之，日卻復中。

translations.literal: After a short while, the sun indeed receded and returned to noon again.
translations.idiomatic: After a short while, the sun indeed receded and returned to noon again.

sentences.zh: 於是始更以十七年為元年，令天下大酺。

translations.literal: So they first changed reckoning, making the seventeenth year Year One, and ordered a realm-wide celebration feast.
translations.idiomatic: So they first changed reckoning, making the seventeenth year Year One, and ordered a realm-wide celebration feast.

sentences.zh: 平言曰：「周鼎亡在泗水中，今河決通於泗，臣望東北汾陰直有金寶氣，意周鼎其出乎？

translations.literal: Ping said: 'The Zhou tripod has long been lost in the Si River. Now the Yellow River breach connects through to the Si, and I observe in the northeast at Fenyin a direct aura of gold and treasure; perhaps the Zhou tripod is about to emerge.
translations.idiomatic: Ping said: 'The Zhou tripod has long been lost in the Si River. Now the Yellow River breach connects through to the Si, and I observe in the northeast at Fenyin a direct aura of gold and treasure; perhaps the Zhou tripod is about to emerge.

sentences.zh: 兆見不迎則不至。」

translations.literal: If an omen appears and we do not welcome it, it will not arrive.'
translations.idiomatic: If an omen appears and we do not welcome it, it will not arrive.'

sentences.zh: 於是上使使治廟汾陰南，臨河，欲祠出周鼎。

translations.literal: So the emperor sent envoys to build a shrine south of Fenyin by the river, intending to sacrifice for the emergence of the Zhou tripod.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor sent envoys to build a shrine south of Fenyin by the river, intending to sacrifice for the emergence of the Zhou tripod.

sentences.zh: 人有上書告平所言皆詐也。

translations.literal: Someone then submitted a memorial accusing Ping's statements of being entirely fraudulent.
translations.idiomatic: Someone then submitted a memorial accusing Ping's statements of being entirely fraudulent.

sentences.zh: 下吏治，誅夷平。

translations.literal: He was handed to judicial officials; Ping and his clan were executed.
translations.idiomatic: He was handed to judicial officials; Ping and his clan were executed.

sentences.zh: 是後，文帝怠於改正服鬼神之事，而渭陽、長門五帝使祠官領，以時致禮，不往焉。

translations.literal: After this, Emperor Wen grew less eager about changing calendar, dress, and ghost-spirit affairs. The Five-Thearch shrines at Weiyang and Changmen were left to sacrificial officers to manage in season, and he no longer went personally.
translations.idiomatic: After this, Emperor Wen grew less eager about changing calendar, dress, and ghost-spirit affairs. The Five-Thearch shrines at Weiyang and Changmen were left to sacrificial officers to manage in season, and he no longer went personally.

sentences.zh: 明年，匈奴數入邊，興兵守御。

translations.literal: The next year, the Xiongnu repeatedly entered the frontier, and troops were raised for defense.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, the Xiongnu repeatedly entered the frontier, and troops were raised for defense.

sentences.zh: 後歲少不登。

translations.literal: In the following year harvests were poor.
translations.idiomatic: In the following year harvests were poor.

sentences.zh: 數歲而孝景即位。

translations.literal: After several years, Emperor Jing came to the throne.
translations.idiomatic: After several years, Emperor Jing came to the throne.

sentences.zh: 十六年，祠官各以歲時祠如故，無有所興。

translations.literal: In his sixteenth year, sacrificial officials continued seasonal rites as before, with no new initiatives.
translations.idiomatic: In his sixteenth year, sacrificial officials continued seasonal rites as before, with no new initiatives.

sentences.zh: 武帝初即位，尤敬鬼神之祀。

translations.literal: When Emperor Wu first took the throne, he was especially reverent toward sacrifices to spirits.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Wu first took the throne, he was especially reverent toward sacrifices to spirits.

sentences.zh: 漢興已六十餘歲矣，天下艾安，縉紳之屬皆望天子封禪改正度也，而上鄉儒術，招賢良。

translations.literal: By then more than sixty years had passed since Han's founding, the realm was generally at peace, and the gentry all hoped the Son of Heaven would perform Feng and Shan rites and reform standards; the emperor turned toward Confucian learning and summoned worthy men.
translations.idiomatic: By then more than sixty years had passed since Han's founding, the realm was generally at peace, and the gentry all hoped the Son of Heaven would perform Feng and Shan rites and reform standards; the emperor turned toward Confucian learning and summoned worthy men.

sentences.zh: 趙綰、王臧等以文學為公卿，欲議古立明堂城南，以朝諸侯，草巡狩封禪改曆服色事未就。

translations.literal: Zhao Wan, Wang Zang, and others rose to high office through literary learning and wanted to discuss restoring an ancient Mingtang south of the city to receive the feudal lords; they drafted plans on inspection tours, Feng and Shan rites, and calendar and dress reforms, but nothing was completed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Wan, Wang Zang, and others rose to high office through literary learning and wanted to discuss restoring an ancient Mingtang south of the city to receive the feudal lords; they drafted plans on inspection tours, Feng and Shan rites, and calendar and dress reforms, but nothing was completed.

sentences.zh: 竇太后不好儒術，使人微伺趙綰等姦利事，按綰、臧，綰、臧自殺，諸所興為皆廢。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou disliked Confucian learning, sent people secretly to watch Zhao Wan and others for corrupt gain, investigated Wan and Zang, and both killed themselves; all these new undertakings were abandoned.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Dowager Dou disliked Confucian learning, sent people secretly to watch Zhao Wan and others for corrupt gain, investigated Wan and Zang, and both killed themselves; all these new undertakings were abandoned.

sentences.zh: 六年，竇太后崩。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Empress Dowager Dou died.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, Empress Dowager Dou died.

sentences.zh: 其明年，徵文學之士。

translations.literal: The next year, men of letters were recruited.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, men of letters were recruited.

sentences.zh: 明年，上初至雍，郊見五畤。

translations.literal: The year after that, the emperor first went to Yong and made suburban audience rites at the five altars.
translations.idiomatic: The year after that, the emperor first went to Yong and made suburban audience rites at the five altars.

sentences.zh: 後常三歲一郊。

translations.literal: Afterward, suburban sacrifice was regularly held once every three years.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, suburban sacrifice was regularly held once every three years.

sentences.zh: 是時上求神君，舍之上林中磃氏館。

translations.literal: At this time the emperor sought the Divine Lord and housed it at the Di clan lodge in Shanglin.
translations.idiomatic: At this time the emperor sought the Divine Lord and housed it at the Di clan lodge in Shanglin.

sentences.zh: 神君者，長陵女子，以乳死，見神於先後宛若。

translations.literal: This Divine Lord had been a woman from Changling who died in childbirth and then appeared as a spirit through the medium Wanruo.
translations.idiomatic: This Divine Lord had been a woman from Changling who died in childbirth and then appeared as a spirit through the medium Wanruo.

sentences.zh: 宛若祠之其室，民多往祠。

translations.literal: Wanruo worshiped it in her chamber, and many common people went there to sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: Wanruo worshiped it in her chamber, and many common people went there to sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 平原君亦往祠，其後子孫以尊顯。

translations.literal: The Lord of Pingyuan also went to sacrifice there, and afterward his descendants rose to rank and prominence.
translations.idiomatic: The Lord of Pingyuan also went to sacrifice there, and afterward his descendants rose to rank and prominence.

sentences.zh: 及上即位，則厚禮置祠之內中。

translations.literal: When the emperor took the throne, he installed the shrine with lavish honors in the inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor took the throne, he installed the shrine with lavish honors in the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 聞其言，不見其人云。

translations.literal: It was said one heard its voice but did not see its form.
translations.idiomatic: It was said one heard its voice but did not see its form.

sentences.zh: 是時，李少君亦以祠灶、穀道、卻老方見上，上尊之。

translations.literal: At this time Li Shaojun also gained audience by methods of stove sacrifice, grain discipline, and anti-aging arts, and the emperor honored him.
translations.idiomatic: At this time Li Shaojun also gained audience by methods of stove sacrifice, grain discipline, and anti-aging arts, and the emperor honored him.

sentences.zh: 少君者，故深澤侯人，主方。

translations.literal: Shaojun was originally from the area of Shenze, a specialist in occult techniques.
translations.idiomatic: Shaojun was originally from the area of Shenze, a specialist in occult techniques.

sentences.zh: 匿其年及所生長。

translations.literal: He concealed his age and birthplace.
translations.idiomatic: He concealed his age and birthplace.

sentences.zh: 常自謂七十，能使物，卻老。

translations.literal: He always claimed to be seventy and said he could command things and reverse aging.
translations.idiomatic: He always claimed to be seventy and said he could command things and reverse aging.

sentences.zh: 其游以方遍諸侯。

translations.literal: He traveled among the feudal courts by means of his techniques.
translations.idiomatic: He traveled among the feudal courts by means of his techniques.

sentences.zh: 無妻子。

translations.literal: He had no wife or children.
translations.idiomatic: He had no wife or children.

sentences.zh: 人聞其能使物及不死，更餽遺之，常餘金錢衣食。

translations.literal: People heard that he could command things and attain immortality, so they kept giving him gifts, and he always had surplus gold, money, clothes, and food.
translations.idiomatic: People heard that he could command things and attain immortality, so they kept giving him gifts, and he always had surplus gold, money, clothes, and food.

sentences.zh: 人皆以為不治產業而饒給，又不知其何所人，愈信，爭事之。

translations.literal: Because he seemed prosperous without managing property and no one knew where he came from, people trusted him more and competed to serve him.
translations.idiomatic: Because he seemed prosperous without managing property and no one knew where he came from, people trusted him more and competed to serve him.

sentences.zh: 少君資好方，善為巧發奇中。

translations.literal: Shaojun was naturally fond of techniques and good at clever improvisations that struck unexpectedly true.
translations.idiomatic: Shaojun was naturally fond of techniques and good at clever improvisations that struck unexpectedly true.

sentences.zh: 常從武安侯宴，坐中有年九十餘老人，少君乃言與其大父游射處，老人為兒從其大父，識其處，一坐盡驚。

translations.literal: At a banquet with the Marquis of Wu'an, there was an old man over ninety; Shaojun said he had once gone shooting with the old man's grandfather, and the old man, who had followed that grandfather as a child, recognized the place - everyone at table was astonished.
translations.idiomatic: At a banquet with the Marquis of Wu'an, there was an old man over ninety; Shaojun said he had once gone shooting with the old man's grandfather, and the old man, who had followed that grandfather as a child, recognized the place - everyone at table was astonished.

sentences.zh: 少君見上，上有故銅器，問少君。

translations.literal: When Shaojun saw the emperor, the emperor showed him an old bronze vessel and asked about it.
translations.idiomatic: When Shaojun saw the emperor, the emperor showed him an old bronze vessel and asked about it.

sentences.zh: 少君曰：「此器齊桓公十年陳於柏寢。」

translations.literal: Shaojun said, 'This vessel was displayed by Duke Huan of Qi in the tenth year, in the Bo chamber.'
translations.idiomatic: Shaojun said, 'This vessel was displayed by Duke Huan of Qi in the tenth year, in the Bo chamber.'

sentences.zh: 已而按其刻，果齊桓公器。

translations.literal: They then checked its inscription, and it was indeed a vessel of Duke Huan of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: They then checked its inscription, and it was indeed a vessel of Duke Huan of Qi.

sentences.zh: 一宮盡駭，以為少君神，數百歲人也。

translations.literal: The whole palace was shocked and took Shaojun for a divine man hundreds of years old.
translations.idiomatic: The whole palace was shocked and took Shaojun for a divine man hundreds of years old.

sentences.zh: 少君言上：「祠灶皆可致物，致物而丹沙可化為黃金，黃金成以為飲食器則益壽，益壽而海中蓬萊僊者乃可見之，以封禪則不死，黃帝是也。

translations.literal: Shaojun told the emperor: 'If one sacrifices to the stove, one can summon things. Once things are summoned, cinnabar can be transformed into gold; when gold is made into eating vessels, lifespan increases; when lifespan increases, the transcendents of Penglai in the sea can be seen; and with Feng and Shan rites one does not die - this is what happened with the Yellow Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: Shaojun told the emperor: 'If one sacrifices to the stove, one can summon things. Once things are summoned, cinnabar can be transformed into gold; when gold is made into eating vessels, lifespan increases; when lifespan increases, the transcendents of Penglai in the sea can be seen; and with Feng and Shan rites one does not die - this is what happened with the Yellow Thearch.

sentences.zh: 臣嘗游海上，見安期生，安期生食臣棗，大如瓜。

translations.literal: I have traveled by sea and seen Anqi Sheng; he fed me jujubes as large as melons.
translations.idiomatic: I have traveled by sea and seen Anqi Sheng; he fed me jujubes as large as melons.

sentences.zh: 安期生僊者，通蓬萊中，合則見人，不合則隱。」

translations.literal: Anqi Sheng is a transcendent connected with Penglai; when conditions align he appears to people, and when they do not he hides.'
translations.idiomatic: Anqi Sheng is a transcendent connected with Penglai; when conditions align he appears to people, and when they do not he hides.'

sentences.zh: 於是天子始親祠灶，遣方士入海求蓬萊安期生之屬，而事化丹沙諸藥齊為黃金矣。

translations.literal: So the Son of Heaven personally began stove sacrifice, sent occult practitioners to sea seeking Penglai and Anqi Sheng, and pursued alchemical medicines for transforming cinnabar into gold.
translations.idiomatic: So the Son of Heaven personally began stove sacrifice, sent occult practitioners to sea seeking Penglai and Anqi Sheng, and pursued alchemical medicines for transforming cinnabar into gold.

sentences.zh: 久之，少君病死。

translations.literal: After a long time, Shaojun fell ill and died.
translations.idiomatic: After a long time, Shaojun fell ill and died.

sentences.zh: 天子以為化去不死也，使黃錘史寬舒受其方，而海上燕齊怪迂之方士多更來言神事矣。

translations.literal: The emperor believed he had transformed and departed without dying, and had Huang Chui's historian Kuan Shu receive his methods; then many strange and eccentric fangshi from the Yan-Qi seacoast came again speaking of spirit matters.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor believed he had transformed and departed without dying, and had Huang Chui's historian Kuan Shu receive his methods; then many strange and eccentric fangshi from the Yan-Qi seacoast came again speaking of spirit matters.

sentences.zh: 亳人謬忌奏祠泰一方，曰：「天神貴者泰一，泰一佐曰五帝。

translations.literal: A man from Bo, Miu Ji, submitted a method for sacrificing to Taiyi, saying: 'Among heavenly gods, the most exalted is Taiyi, whose assistants are the Five Thearchs.
translations.idiomatic: A man from Bo, Miu Ji, submitted a method for sacrificing to Taiyi, saying: 'Among heavenly gods, the most exalted is Taiyi, whose assistants are the Five Thearchs.

sentences.zh: 古者天子以春秋祭泰一東南郊，日一太牢，七日，為壇開八通之鬼道。」

translations.literal: In antiquity, the Son of Heaven in spring and autumn sacrificed to Taiyi at the southeastern suburb, one full ox per day for seven days, with an altar opening eight spirit pathways.'
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, the Son of Heaven in spring and autumn sacrificed to Taiyi at the southeastern suburb, one full ox per day for seven days, with an altar opening eight spirit pathways.'

sentences.zh: 於是，天子令太祝立其祠長安城東南郊，常奉祠如忌方。

translations.literal: So the emperor ordered the Grand Invocator to establish this shrine in the southeast suburb of Chang'an and to maintain regular rites according to Ji's method.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor ordered the Grand Invocator to establish this shrine in the southeast suburb of Chang'an and to maintain regular rites according to Ji's method.

sentences.zh: 其後，人上書言「古者天子三年一用太牢祠三一：天一、地一、泰一。」

translations.literal: Later someone memorialized: 'In antiquity the Son of Heaven every three years used one full ox to sacrifice to the Three Ones: Heavenly One, Earthly One, and Taiyi.'
translations.idiomatic: Later someone memorialized: 'In antiquity the Son of Heaven every three years used one full ox to sacrifice to the Three Ones: Heavenly One, Earthly One, and Taiyi.'

sentences.zh: 天子許之，令太祝領祠之於忌泰一壇上，如其方。

translations.literal: The emperor approved and ordered the Grand Invocator to manage this on Ji's Taiyi altar according to the method.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved and ordered the Grand Invocator to manage this on Ji's Taiyi altar according to the method.

sentences.zh: 後人復有言「古天子常以春解祠，祠黃帝用一梟、破鏡；

translations.literal: Later someone again said: 'Ancient Sons of Heaven often held spring-release rites: for the Yellow Thearch they used one owl and one shattered mirror;
translations.idiomatic: Later someone again said: 'Ancient Sons of Heaven often held spring-release rites: for the Yellow Thearch they used one owl and one shattered mirror;

sentences.zh: 冥羊用羊祠；

translations.literal: for Mingyang they used sheep sacrifice;
translations.idiomatic: for Mingyang they used sheep sacrifice;

sentences.zh: 馬行用一青牡馬；

translations.literal: for Horse-Procession they used one blue stallion;
translations.idiomatic: for Horse-Procession they used one blue stallion;

sentences.zh: 泰一、皋山山君用牛；

translations.literal: for Taiyi and the Lord of Gaoshan they used oxen;
translations.idiomatic: for Taiyi and the Lord of Gaoshan they used oxen;

sentences.zh: 武夷君用乾魚；

translations.literal: for the Lord of Wuyi they used dried fish;
translations.idiomatic: for the Lord of Wuyi they used dried fish;

sentences.zh: 陰陽使者以一牛。」

translations.literal: for Yin-Yang messengers they used one ox.'
translations.idiomatic: for Yin-Yang messengers they used one ox.'

sentences.zh: 令祠官領之如其方，而祠泰一於忌泰一壇旁。

translations.literal: He ordered sacrificial officers to carry this out accordingly, with Taiyi sacrificed beside Ji's Taiyi altar.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered sacrificial officers to carry this out accordingly, with Taiyi sacrificed beside Ji's Taiyi altar.

sentences.zh: 後二年，郊雍，獲一角獸，若麃然。

translations.literal: Two years later, during suburban rites at Yong, they captured a one-horned beast, somewhat like a roe deer.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, during suburban rites at Yong, they captured a one-horned beast, somewhat like a roe deer.

sentences.zh: 有司曰：「陛下肅祗郊祀，上帝報享，錫一角獸，蓋麟云。」

translations.literal: Officials said: 'Your Majesty has reverently performed suburban rites; the High Thearch has accepted and rewarded you with a one-horned beast, likely a qilin.'
translations.idiomatic: Officials said: 'Your Majesty has reverently performed suburban rites; the High Thearch has accepted and rewarded you with a one-horned beast, likely a qilin.'

sentences.zh: 於是以薦五畤，畤加一牛以燎。

translations.literal: So it was offered at the five altars, each altar adding one ox for the burnt offering.
translations.idiomatic: So it was offered at the five altars, each altar adding one ox for the burnt offering.

sentences.zh: 賜諸侯白金，以風符應合於天也。

translations.literal: White metal was bestowed on the feudal lords to proclaim that responsive signs matched Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: White metal was bestowed on the feudal lords to proclaim that responsive signs matched Heaven.

sentences.zh: 於是濟北王以為天子且封禪，上書獻泰山及其旁邑，天子以它縣償之。

translations.literal: Then the King of Jibei, thinking the Son of Heaven was about to perform Feng and Shan rites, memorialized to offer Mount Tai and nearby towns; the emperor compensated him with other counties.
translations.idiomatic: Then the King of Jibei, thinking the Son of Heaven was about to perform Feng and Shan rites, memorialized to offer Mount Tai and nearby towns; the emperor compensated him with other counties.

sentences.zh: 常山王有罪，俣，天子封其弟真定，以續先王祀，而以常山為郡。

translations.literal: The King of Changshan committed an offense and died; the emperor enfeoffed his younger brother at Zhending to continue the ancestral royal sacrifices, and made Changshan a commandery.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Changshan committed an offense and died; the emperor enfeoffed his younger brother at Zhending to continue the ancestral royal sacrifices, and made Changshan a commandery.

sentences.zh: 然後五嶽皆在天子之郡。

translations.literal: Only then were all Five Peaks within commanderies of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Only then were all Five Peaks within commanderies of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 明年，齊人少翁以方見上。

translations.literal: The next year, a man of Qi named Shaoweng gained audience through occult techniques.
translations.idiomatic: The next year, a man of Qi named Shaoweng gained audience through occult techniques.

sentences.zh: 上有所幸李夫人，夫人卒，少翁以方蓋夜致夫人及灶鬼之貌云，天子自帷中望見焉。

translations.literal: The emperor had once favored Lady Li; after she died, Shaoweng by his methods reportedly summoned her image and that of the stove spirit at night, and the emperor saw them from behind curtains.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had once favored Lady Li; after she died, Shaoweng by his methods reportedly summoned her image and that of the stove spirit at night, and the emperor saw them from behind curtains.

sentences.zh: 乃拜少翁為文成將軍，賞賜甚多，以客禮禮之。

translations.literal: He then appointed Shaoweng General Wencheng, gave him lavish rewards, and treated him with guest honors.
translations.idiomatic: He then appointed Shaoweng General Wencheng, gave him lavish rewards, and treated him with guest honors.

sentences.zh: 文成言：「上即欲與神通，宮室被服非象神，神物不至。」

translations.literal: Wencheng said: 'If Your Majesty truly wishes to communicate with spirits, palace buildings and dress must resemble those of spirits, or spirit beings will not come.'
translations.idiomatic: Wencheng said: 'If Your Majesty truly wishes to communicate with spirits, palace buildings and dress must resemble those of spirits, or spirit beings will not come.'

sentences.zh: 乃作畫雲氣車，及各以勝日駕車辟惡鬼。

translations.literal: So they made cloud-patterned ritual carriages and drove them on prescribed days to ward off evil spirits.
translations.idiomatic: So they made cloud-patterned ritual carriages and drove them on prescribed days to ward off evil spirits.

sentences.zh: 又作甘泉宮，中為臺室，畫天地泰一諸鬼神，而置祭具以致天神。

translations.literal: He also built Ganquan Palace with a terrace chamber, painted with Heaven, Earth, Taiyi, and various spirit beings, and set up sacrificial furnishings to attract heavenly gods.
translations.idiomatic: He also built Ganquan Palace with a terrace chamber, painted with Heaven, Earth, Taiyi, and various spirit beings, and set up sacrificial furnishings to attract heavenly gods.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，其方益衰，神不至。

translations.literal: After more than a year, however, his methods declined and spirits did not come.
translations.idiomatic: After more than a year, however, his methods declined and spirits did not come.

sentences.zh: 乃為帛書以飯牛，陽不知，言此牛腹中有奇書。

translations.literal: So he wrote on silk and fed it to a cow, pretending ignorance and saying there was a strange text in the cow's belly.
translations.idiomatic: So he wrote on silk and fed it to a cow, pretending ignorance and saying there was a strange text in the cow's belly.

sentences.zh: 殺視得書，書言甚怪。

translations.literal: They slaughtered it and found the text, whose message was highly bizarre.
translations.idiomatic: They slaughtered it and found the text, whose message was highly bizarre.

sentences.zh: 天子識其手，問之，果為書。

translations.literal: The emperor recognized his handwriting, questioned him, and it was indeed written by him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor recognized his handwriting, questioned him, and it was indeed written by him.

sentences.zh: 於是誅文成將軍，隱之。

translations.literal: So General Wencheng was executed and the matter concealed.
translations.idiomatic: So General Wencheng was executed and the matter concealed.

sentences.zh: 其後又作柏梁、銅柱、承露僊人掌之屬矣。

translations.literal: Afterward they also built things such as Boliang Terrace, bronze pillars, and immortal palms for receiving dew.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward they also built things such as Boliang Terrace, bronze pillars, and immortal palms for receiving dew.

sentences.zh: 文成死明年，天子病鼎湖甚，巫醫無所不致。

translations.literal: The year after Wencheng's death, the emperor fell gravely ill at Dinghu, and every sort of shaman and physician was brought in.
translations.idiomatic: The year after Wencheng's death, the emperor fell gravely ill at Dinghu, and every sort of shaman and physician was brought in.

sentences.zh: 游水發根言上郡有巫，病而鬼下之。

translations.literal: A man named You Shuifagen said there was a shaman in Shang commandery who, when ill, could bring spirits down.
translations.idiomatic: A man named You Shuifagen said there was a shaman in Shang commandery who, when ill, could bring spirits down.

sentences.zh: 上召置祠之甘泉。

translations.literal: The emperor summoned him and established his shrine at Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned him and established his shrine at Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 及病，使人問神君，神君言曰：「天子無憂病。

translations.literal: During illness, the emperor sent to ask the Divine Lord, and the Divine Lord said: 'The Son of Heaven need not worry about this illness.
translations.idiomatic: During illness, the emperor sent to ask the Divine Lord, and the Divine Lord said: 'The Son of Heaven need not worry about this illness.

sentences.zh: 病少瘉，強與我會甘泉。」

translations.literal: When you are somewhat recovered, come forcefully to meet me at Ganquan.'
translations.idiomatic: When you are somewhat recovered, come forcefully to meet me at Ganquan.'

sentences.zh: 於是上病瘉，遂起，幸甘泉，病良已。

translations.literal: The emperor's illness improved, he rose, traveled to Ganquan, and the illness was fully cured.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's illness improved, he rose, traveled to Ganquan, and the illness was fully cured.

sentences.zh: 大赦，置壽宮神君。

translations.literal: A general amnesty was proclaimed, and a longevity palace for the Divine Lord was established.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed, and a longevity palace for the Divine Lord was established.

sentences.zh: 神君最貴者曰太一，其佐曰太禁、司命之屬，皆從之。

translations.literal: Among the Divine Lord's most honored figures was Taiyi; its assistants were called Taijin, Siming, and others, all accompanying it.
translations.idiomatic: Among the Divine Lord's most honored figures was Taiyi; its assistants were called Taijin, Siming, and others, all accompanying it.

sentences.zh: 非可得見，聞其言，言與人音等。

translations.literal: They could not be seen directly; one only heard their voices, as if equal to human speech.
translations.idiomatic: They could not be seen directly; one only heard their voices, as if equal to human speech.

sentences.zh: 時去時來，來則風肅然。

translations.literal: At times they left and at times came; when they came, a solemn wind arose.
translations.idiomatic: At times they left and at times came; when they came, a solemn wind arose.

sentences.zh: 居室帷中，時晝言，然常以夜。

translations.literal: They stayed behind room curtains, sometimes speaking by day though usually at night.
translations.idiomatic: They stayed behind room curtains, sometimes speaking by day though usually at night.

sentences.zh: 天子祓，然後入。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven performed purification rites before entering.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven performed purification rites before entering.

sentences.zh: 因巫為主人，關飲食，所欲言，行下。

translations.literal: Through the shaman as host, rules were set for food and drink, and whatever was desired to be said was conveyed downward.
translations.idiomatic: Through the shaman as host, rules were set for food and drink, and whatever was desired to be said was conveyed downward.

sentences.zh: 又置壽宮、北宮，張羽旗，設共具，以禮神君。

translations.literal: He also established longevity and northern palaces, hung feather banners, and set ritual furnishings to honor the Divine Lord.
translations.idiomatic: He also established longevity and northern palaces, hung feather banners, and set ritual furnishings to honor the Divine Lord.

sentences.zh: 神君所言，上使受書，其名曰「畫法」。

translations.literal: What the Divine Lord said was written down by imperial order and called the 'Methods of Images.'
translations.idiomatic: What the Divine Lord said was written down by imperial order and called the 'Methods of Images.'

sentences.zh: 其所言，世俗之所知也，無絕殊者，而天子心獨憙。

translations.literal: Its contents were things ordinary people already knew, nothing uniquely extraordinary, yet the Son of Heaven alone was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Its contents were things ordinary people already knew, nothing uniquely extraordinary, yet the Son of Heaven alone was greatly pleased.

sentences.zh: 其事祕，世莫知也。

translations.literal: The affair remained secret, unknown to the world.
translations.idiomatic: The affair remained secret, unknown to the world.

sentences.zh: 後三年，有司言元宜以天瑞，不宜以一二數。

translations.literal: Three years later, officials said era names should follow heavenly auspices and not merely count one, two, three.
translations.idiomatic: Three years later, officials said era names should follow heavenly auspices and not merely count one, two, three.

sentences.zh: 一元曰「建」，二元以長星曰「光」，今郊得一角獸曰「狩」云。

translations.literal: The first era had been called 'Jian'; the second, from a long star, 'Guang'; now with the suburban capture of a one-horned beast, it should be called 'Shou.'
translations.idiomatic: The first era had been called 'Jian'; the second, from a long star, 'Guang'; now with the suburban capture of a one-horned beast, it should be called 'Shou.'

sentences.zh: 其明年，天子郊雍，曰：「今上帝朕親郊，而后土無祀，則禮不答也。」

translations.literal: The next year, when the Son of Heaven held suburban rites at Yong, he said: 'Now I personally sacrifice to the High Thearch, yet Houtu has no sacrifice; this is ritually unbalanced.'
translations.idiomatic: The next year, when the Son of Heaven held suburban rites at Yong, he said: 'Now I personally sacrifice to the High Thearch, yet Houtu has no sacrifice; this is ritually unbalanced.'

sentences.zh: 有司與太史令談、祠官寬舒議：「天地牲，角繭栗。

translations.literal: Officials, together with Grand Historian Ling Tan and sacrificial officer Kuan Shu, discussed: 'For Heaven and Earth rites, victims should have horns and be chestnut-brown.
translations.idiomatic: Officials, together with Grand Historian Ling Tan and sacrificial officer Kuan Shu, discussed: 'For Heaven and Earth rites, victims should have horns and be chestnut-brown.

sentences.zh: 今陛下親祠后土，后土宜於澤中圜丘為五壇，壇一黃犢牢具。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty personally sacrifices to Houtu, Houtu should be worshiped on round mounds in marshland as five altars, each with one yellow calf and full sacrificial set.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty personally sacrifices to Houtu, Houtu should be worshiped on round mounds in marshland as five altars, each with one yellow calf and full sacrificial set.

sentences.zh: 已祠盡瘞，而從祠衣上黃。」

translations.literal: After sacrifice all should be fully buried, and accompanying sacrificial dress should honor yellow.'
translations.idiomatic: After sacrifice all should be fully buried, and accompanying sacrificial dress should honor yellow.'

sentences.zh: 於是天子東幸汾陰。

translations.literal: So the Son of Heaven traveled east to Fenyin.
translations.idiomatic: So the Son of Heaven traveled east to Fenyin.

sentences.zh: 汾陰男子公孫滂洋等見汾旁有光如絳，上遂立后土祠於汾陰脽上，如寬舒等議。

translations.literal: Men of Fenyin, Gongsun Pangyang and others, reported seeing crimson-like light by the Fen River, and the emperor accordingly established a Houtu shrine on the Fenyin mound as Kuan Shu and others had proposed.
translations.idiomatic: Men of Fenyin, Gongsun Pangyang and others, reported seeing crimson-like light by the Fen River, and the emperor accordingly established a Houtu shrine on the Fenyin mound as Kuan Shu and others had proposed.

sentences.zh: 上親望拜，如上帝禮。

translations.literal: The emperor personally offered distant bows, following the rites for the High Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor personally offered distant bows, following the rites for the High Thearch.

sentences.zh: 禮畢，天子遂至滎陽。

translations.literal: When rites were completed, the emperor proceeded to Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: When rites were completed, the emperor proceeded to Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 還過雒陽，下詔封周後，令奉其祀。

translations.literal: Returning by Luoyang, he issued an edict enfeoffing Zhou descendants and ordering them to maintain Zhou sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Returning by Luoyang, he issued an edict enfeoffing Zhou descendants and ordering them to maintain Zhou sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 語在武紀。

translations.literal: This is recorded in the Annals of Emperor Wu.
translations.idiomatic: This is recorded in the Annals of Emperor Wu.

sentences.zh: 上始巡幸郡縣，寖尋於泰山矣。

translations.literal: This was when the emperor began inspection tours of commanderies and counties, gradually directing his search toward Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: This was when the emperor began inspection tours of commanderies and counties, gradually directing his search toward Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 其春，樂成侯登上書言欒大。

translations.literal: That spring, Marquis Lecheng Deng submitted a memorial about Luan Da.
translations.idiomatic: That spring, Marquis Lecheng Deng submitted a memorial about Luan Da.

sentences.zh: 欒大，膠東宮人，故嘗與文成將軍同師，已而為膠東王尚方。

translations.literal: Luan Da, a palace retainer from Jiaodong, had formerly studied under the same master as General Wencheng and later served as artisan-official to the King of Jiaodong.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Da, a palace retainer from Jiaodong, had formerly studied under the same master as General Wencheng and later served as artisan-official to the King of Jiaodong.

sentences.zh: 而樂成侯姊為康王后，無子。

translations.literal: And Marquis Lecheng's elder sister was Queen Consort of King Kang, but she had no son.
translations.idiomatic: And Marquis Lecheng's elder sister was Queen Consort of King Kang, but she had no son.

sentences.zh: 王死，它姬子立為王，而康后有淫行，與王不相中，相危以法。

translations.literal: When the king died, a son by another consort was enthroned; Queen Kang had an illicit affair, was out of favor with the king, and both sides threatened each other through legal charges.
translations.idiomatic: When the king died, a son by another consort was enthroned; Queen Kang had an illicit affair, was out of favor with the king, and both sides threatened each other through legal charges.

sentences.zh: 康后聞文成死，而欲自媚於上，乃遣欒大入，因樂成侯求見言方。

translations.literal: When Queen Kang heard Wencheng had died and wanted to ingratiate herself with the emperor, she sent Luan Da in and used Marquis Lecheng to seek audience by speaking of occult methods.
translations.idiomatic: When Queen Kang heard Wencheng had died and wanted to ingratiate herself with the emperor, she sent Luan Da in and used Marquis Lecheng to seek audience by speaking of occult methods.

sentences.zh: 天子既誅文成，後悔其方不盡，及見欒大，大說。

translations.literal: Since the Son of Heaven had executed Wencheng, he later regretted not exhausting his techniques; when he met Luan Da, he was greatly pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Son of Heaven had executed Wencheng, he later regretted not exhausting his techniques; when he met Luan Da, he was greatly pleased.

sentences.zh: 大為人長美，言多方略，而敢為大言，處之不疑。

translations.literal: Luan Da was tall and handsome, spoke of many strategic methods, dared make grand claims, and carried himself without hesitation.
translations.idiomatic: Luan Da was tall and handsome, spoke of many strategic methods, dared make grand claims, and carried himself without hesitation.

sentences.zh: 大言曰：「臣常往來海中，見安期、羨門之屬，顧以臣為賤，不信臣。

translations.literal: Da said: 'I have often traveled at sea and seen people like Anqi and Xianmen, but they look down on me as low and do not trust me.
translations.idiomatic: Da said: 'I have often traveled at sea and seen people like Anqi and Xianmen, but they look down on me as low and do not trust me.

sentences.zh: 又以為康王諸侯耳，不足與方。

translations.literal: They also think King Kang is only a feudal lord, not worthy to discuss methods with.
translations.idiomatic: They also think King Kang is only a feudal lord, not worthy to discuss methods with.

sentences.zh: 臣數以言康王，康王又不用臣。

translations.literal: I repeatedly spoke to King Kang, but he did not employ me.
translations.idiomatic: I repeatedly spoke to King Kang, but he did not employ me.

sentences.zh: 臣之師曰：『黃金可成，而河決可塞，不死之藥可得，僊人可致也。』

translations.literal: My master said: "Gold can be made, river breaches can be blocked, the medicine of immortality can be obtained, and transcendents can be summoned."
translations.idiomatic: My master said: "Gold can be made, river breaches can be blocked, the medicine of immortality can be obtained, and transcendents can be summoned."

sentences.zh: 然臣恐效文成，則方士皆掩口，惡敢言方哉！」

translations.literal: But I fear ending like Wencheng; then all fangshi will seal their mouths, who would dare speak of methods?'
translations.idiomatic: But I fear ending like Wencheng; then all fangshi will seal their mouths, who would dare speak of methods?'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「

translations.literal: The emperor said:
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said:

sentences.zh: 文成食馬肝死耳。

translations.literal: 'Wencheng only died from eating horse liver.
translations.idiomatic: 'Wencheng only died from eating horse liver.

sentences.zh: 子誠能修其方，我何愛乎！」

translations.literal: If you can truly complete your methods, what would I begrudge?'
translations.idiomatic: If you can truly complete your methods, what would I begrudge?'

sentences.zh: 大曰：「臣師非有求人，人者求之。

translations.literal: Da said: 'My masters do not seek people; people seek them.
translations.idiomatic: Da said: 'My masters do not seek people; people seek them.

sentences.zh: 陛下必欲致之，則貴其使者，令為親屬，以客禮待之，勿卑，使各佩其信印，乃可使通言於神人。

translations.literal: If Your Majesty truly wants to bring them, honor their envoys, make them imperial kin, treat them as guests, and do not abase them; let each wear his credential seal, and only then can they convey words to spirit-men.
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty truly wants to bring them, honor their envoys, make them imperial kin, treat them as guests, and do not abase them; let each wear his credential seal, and only then can they convey words to spirit-men.

sentences.zh: 神人尚肯邪不邪，尊其使然後可致也。」

translations.literal: Whether spirit-men are willing depends on this: honor their envoys first, then they may be brought.'
translations.idiomatic: Whether spirit-men are willing depends on this: honor their envoys first, then they may be brought.'

sentences.zh: 於是上使驗小方，鬥棋，棋自相觸擊。

translations.literal: So the emperor had him test minor techniques: board-game pieces were made to strike each other on their own.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor had him test minor techniques: board-game pieces were made to strike each other on their own.

sentences.zh: 是時，上方憂河決而黃金不就，乃拜大為五利將軍。

translations.literal: At that time the emperor was worried about the river breach and that alchemy had not succeeded, so he appointed Da General of Five Benefits.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the emperor was worried about the river breach and that alchemy had not succeeded, so he appointed Da General of Five Benefits.

sentences.zh: 居月餘，得四印；

translations.literal: After a little over a month, he received four seals.
translations.idiomatic: After a little over a month, he received four seals.

sentences.zh: 得天士將軍、地士將軍、大通將軍印。

translations.literal: He gained the seals of General of Heavenly Scholars, General of Earthly Scholars, and General of Great Penetration.
translations.idiomatic: He gained the seals of General of Heavenly Scholars, General of Earthly Scholars, and General of Great Penetration.

sentences.zh: 制詔御史：「昔禹疏九河，決四瀆。

translations.literal: An edict to the Censor said: 'In former times Yu dredged the nine rivers and opened the four waterways.
translations.idiomatic: An edict to the Censor said: 'In former times Yu dredged the nine rivers and opened the four waterways.

sentences.zh: 間者，河溢皋陸，隄繇不息。

translations.literal: Recently the river has overflowed high ground and dike labor has not ceased.
translations.idiomatic: Recently the river has overflowed high ground and dike labor has not ceased.

sentences.zh: 朕臨天下二十有八年，天若遺朕士而大通焉。

translations.literal: I have ruled the realm twenty-eight years; Heaven seems to have granted me this man and with him great access.
translations.idiomatic: I have ruled the realm twenty-eight years; Heaven seems to have granted me this man and with him great access.

sentences.zh: 乾稱『飛龍』，『鴻漸于般』，朕意庶幾與焉。

translations.literal: Qian says "flying dragon" and "the wild goose gradually alights on the high land"; I hope to share in that meaning.
translations.idiomatic: Qian says "flying dragon" and "the wild goose gradually alights on the high land"; I hope to share in that meaning.

sentences.zh: 其以二千戶封地士將軍大為樂通侯。」

translations.literal: Enfeoff General of Earthly Scholars Da with two thousand households as Marquis Letong.'
translations.idiomatic: Enfeoff General of Earthly Scholars Da with two thousand households as Marquis Letong.'

sentences.zh: 賜列侯甲第，童千人。

translations.literal: He was granted a grand marquis mansion and a thousand servants.
translations.idiomatic: He was granted a grand marquis mansion and a thousand servants.

sentences.zh: 乘輿斥車馬帷帳器物以充其家。

translations.literal: Imperial stores released chariots, horses, curtains, tents, and goods to fill his household.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial stores released chariots, horses, curtains, tents, and goods to fill his household.

sentences.zh: 又以衛長公主妻之，齎金十萬斤，更名其邑曰當利公主。

translations.literal: The emperor also married him to Princess Wei the Elder and gave a dowry of one hundred thousand jin of gold, renaming her fief Princess Dangli.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor also married him to Princess Wei the Elder and gave a dowry of one hundred thousand jin of gold, renaming her fief Princess Dangli.

sentences.zh: 天子親如五利之弟，使者存問共給，相屬於道。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven personally went to Five Benefits' younger brothers; envoys with visits and supplies lined the roads.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven personally went to Five Benefits' younger brothers; envoys with visits and supplies lined the roads.

sentences.zh: 自大主將相以下，皆置酒其家，獻遺之。

translations.literal: From grand princesses, chancellors, and generals downward, all held banquets at his house and gave him gifts.
translations.idiomatic: From grand princesses, chancellors, and generals downward, all held banquets at his house and gave him gifts.

sentences.zh: 天子又刻玉印曰「天道將軍」，使使衣羽衣，夜立白茅上，五利將軍亦衣羽衣，立白茅上受印，以視不臣也。

translations.literal: The emperor also carved a jade seal reading "General of the Heavenly Way," had an envoy wear feather garments and stand at night on white thatch, while General Five Benefits likewise wore feathers and stood on white thatch to receive the seal, to show he was no mere subject.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor also carved a jade seal reading "General of the Heavenly Way," had an envoy wear feather garments and stand at night on white thatch, while General Five Benefits likewise wore feathers and stood on white thatch to receive the seal, to show he was no mere subject.

sentences.zh: 而佩「天道」者，且為天子道天神也。

translations.literal: And one bearing the Heavenly Way seal was to guide heavenly gods on behalf of the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: And one bearing the Heavenly Way seal was to guide heavenly gods on behalf of the Son of Heaven.

sentences.zh: 於是五利常夜祠其家，欲以下神。

translations.literal: Thereupon Five Benefits regularly held night sacrifices at home, seeking to bring spirits down.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon Five Benefits regularly held night sacrifices at home, seeking to bring spirits down.

sentences.zh: 後裝治行，東入海求其師云。

translations.literal: Later he prepared for travel and went east to sea to seek his master, so he said.
translations.idiomatic: Later he prepared for travel and went east to sea to seek his master, so he said.

sentences.zh: 大見數月，佩六印，貴震天下，而海上燕齊之間，莫不搤掔而自言有禁方能神僊矣。

translations.literal: Within a few months Da wore six seals, his prestige shaking the realm, and along the Yan-Qi seacoast everyone struck fists and declared they had secret formulas for divine transcendence.
translations.idiomatic: Within a few months Da wore six seals, his prestige shaking the realm, and along the Yan-Qi seacoast everyone struck fists and declared they had secret formulas for divine transcendence.

sentences.zh: 其夏六月，汾陰巫錦為民祠魏脽后土營旁，見地如鉤狀，掊視得鼎。

translations.literal: That summer in the sixth month, at Fenyin, a shaman named Jin was conducting a common rite beside the Wei mound Houtu precinct and saw ground shaped like a hook; digging there, he found a tripod.
translations.idiomatic: That summer in the sixth month, at Fenyin, a shaman named Jin was conducting a common rite beside the Wei mound Houtu precinct and saw ground shaped like a hook; digging there, he found a tripod.

sentences.zh: 鼎大異於眾鼎，文鏤無款識，怪之，言吏。

translations.literal: The tripod was strikingly different from ordinary tripods, with carved patterns but no inscription, and he found it strange and reported it to officials.
translations.idiomatic: The tripod was strikingly different from ordinary tripods, with carved patterns but no inscription, and he found it strange and reported it to officials.

sentences.zh: 吏告河東太守勝，勝以聞。

translations.literal: Officials reported it to He Sheng, governor of Hedong, and Sheng reported it upward.
translations.idiomatic: Officials reported it to He Sheng, governor of Hedong, and Sheng reported it upward.

sentences.zh: 天子使驗問巫得鼎無姦詐，乃以禮祠，迎鼎至甘泉，從上行，薦之。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven ordered investigation to confirm the shaman found the tripod without fraud; then he had it ritually worshiped and escorted to Ganquan, accompanying the emperor's route and offered it.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven ordered investigation to confirm the shaman found the tripod without fraud; then he had it ritually worshiped and escorted to Ganquan, accompanying the emperor's route and offered it.

sentences.zh: 至中山，晏溫，有黃雲焉。

translations.literal: Arriving at Zhongshan, the weather was mild and warm, and yellow clouds appeared.
translations.idiomatic: Arriving at Zhongshan, the weather was mild and warm, and yellow clouds appeared.

sentences.zh: 有鹿過，上自射之，因之以祭云。

translations.literal: A deer passed by; the emperor shot it himself and used it for sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: A deer passed by; the emperor shot it himself and used it for sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 至長安，公卿大夫皆議尊寶鼎。

translations.literal: When they reached Chang'an, high officials all debated honoring the precious tripod.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached Chang'an, high officials all debated honoring the precious tripod.

sentences.zh: 天子曰：「間者河溢，歲數不登，故巡祭后土，祈為百姓育穀。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven said: 'Recently the river overflowed and harvests repeatedly failed, so I toured and sacrificed to Houtu to pray for grain growth for the people.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven said: 'Recently the river overflowed and harvests repeatedly failed, so I toured and sacrificed to Houtu to pray for grain growth for the people.

sentences.zh: 今年豐楙未報，鼎曷為出哉？」

translations.literal: This year's rich growth has not yet repaid that prayer, why then has the tripod appeared?'
translations.idiomatic: This year's rich growth has not yet repaid that prayer, why then has the tripod appeared?'

sentences.zh: 有司皆言：「聞昔泰帝興神鼎一，一者一統，天地萬物所繫象也。

translations.literal: Officials all said: 'We have heard that when the Great Thearch arose, one divine tripod appeared; one signifies unified rule and the governing pattern of Heaven, Earth, and all things.
translations.idiomatic: Officials all said: 'We have heard that when the Great Thearch arose, one divine tripod appeared; one signifies unified rule and the governing pattern of Heaven, Earth, and all things.

sentences.zh: 黃帝作寶鼎三，象天地人。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch made three precious tripods, symbolizing Heaven, Earth, and humanity.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch made three precious tripods, symbolizing Heaven, Earth, and humanity.

sentences.zh: 禹收九牧之金，鑄九鼎，象九州。

translations.literal: Yu gathered metal from the nine provinces and cast nine tripods, symbolizing the nine regions.
translations.idiomatic: Yu gathered metal from the nine provinces and cast nine tripods, symbolizing the nine regions.

sentences.zh: 皆嘗鬺享上帝鬼神。

translations.literal: All were once used in offerings to the High Thearch and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: All were once used in offerings to the High Thearch and spirits.

sentences.zh: 其空足曰鬲，以象三德，饗承天祜。

translations.literal: Their hollow legs were called li, symbolizing three virtues and receiving Heaven's blessing in offerings.
translations.idiomatic: Their hollow legs were called li, symbolizing three virtues and receiving Heaven's blessing in offerings.

sentences.zh: 夏德衰，鼎遷於殷；

translations.literal: When Xia virtue declined, the tripods moved to Yin.
translations.idiomatic: When Xia virtue declined, the tripods moved to Yin.

sentences.zh: 殷德衰，鼎遷於周；

translations.literal: When Yin virtue declined, they moved to Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: When Yin virtue declined, they moved to Zhou.

sentences.zh: 周德衰，鼎遷於秦；

translations.literal: When Zhou virtue declined, they moved to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou virtue declined, they moved to Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦德衰，宋之社亡，鼎乃淪伏而不見。

translations.literal: When Qin virtue declined and the Song altar perished, the tripods then sank and disappeared.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin virtue declined and the Song altar perished, the tripods then sank and disappeared.

sentences.zh: 周頌曰：『自堂徂基，自羊徂牛，鼐鼎及鼒；

translations.literal: A Zhou hymn says: "From hall to foundation, from sheep to ox, from great tripods to lesser tripods.
translations.idiomatic: A Zhou hymn says: "From hall to foundation, from sheep to ox, from great tripods to lesser tripods.

sentences.zh: 不羁不敖，胡考之休。』

translations.literal: Unfettered, unarrogant, this is lasting blessedness."
translations.idiomatic: Unfettered, unarrogant, this is lasting blessedness."

sentences.zh: 今鼎至甘泉，以光潤龍變，承休無疆。

translations.literal: Now the tripod has come to Ganquan, with shining luster and dragon-like transformation, bearing endless blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Now the tripod has come to Ganquan, with shining luster and dragon-like transformation, bearing endless blessing.

sentences.zh: 合茲中山，有黃白雲降，蓋若獸為符，路弓乘矢，集獲壇下，報祠大亨。

translations.literal: Along the way at Zhongshan, yellow-white clouds descended; there was also an animal omen. On the road, bow and riding arrows were gathered beneath the altar, repaying sacrifice with great fulfillment.
translations.idiomatic: Along the way at Zhongshan, yellow-white clouds descended; there was also an animal omen. On the road, bow and riding arrows were gathered beneath the altar, repaying sacrifice with great fulfillment.

sentences.zh: 唯受命而帝者心知其意而合德焉。

translations.literal: Only one who has received the mandate and reigns as emperor knows this meaning in his heart and matches it in virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Only one who has received the mandate and reigns as emperor knows this meaning in his heart and matches it in virtue.

sentences.zh: 鼎宜視宗禰廣，臧於帝庭，以合明應。」

translations.literal: The tripod should receive temple-level honors, be stored in the imperial court, and thereby accord with this manifest response.'
translations.idiomatic: The tripod should receive temple-level honors, be stored in the imperial court, and thereby accord with this manifest response.'

sentences.zh: 制曰：「可。」

translations.literal: The decree said: 'Approved.'
translations.idiomatic: The decree said: 'Approved.'

sentences.zh: 入海求蓬萊者，言蓬萊不遠，而不能至者，殆不見其氣。

translations.literal: Those sent to sea to seek Penglai said Penglai was not far, and that failure to reach it was likely because they could not perceive its qi.
translations.idiomatic: Those sent to sea to seek Penglai said Penglai was not far, and that failure to reach it was likely because they could not perceive its qi.

sentences.zh: 上乃遣望氣佐候其氣云。

translations.literal: So the emperor sent qi-observers to wait for and watch those vapors.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor sent qi-observers to wait for and watch those vapors.

sentences.zh: 其秋，上雍，且郊。

translations.literal: That autumn, the emperor went to Yong and was about to hold suburban sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, the emperor went to Yong and was about to hold suburban sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 或曰「五帝，泰一之佐也，宜立泰一而上親郊之」。

translations.literal: Someone said: 'The Five Thearchs are assistants to Taiyi; Taiyi should be established and personally worshiped by the emperor in suburban rite.'
translations.idiomatic: Someone said: 'The Five Thearchs are assistants to Taiyi; Taiyi should be established and personally worshiped by the emperor in suburban rite.'

sentences.zh: 上疑未定。

translations.literal: The emperor was uncertain and had not decided.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was uncertain and had not decided.

sentences.zh: 齊人公孫卿曰：「今年得寶鼎，其冬辛巳朔旦冬至，與黃帝時等。」

translations.literal: A man of Qi, Gongsun Qing, said: 'This year we obtained a precious tripod, and this winter's xin-si new moon dawn at winter solstice matches the time of the Yellow Thearch.'
translations.idiomatic: A man of Qi, Gongsun Qing, said: 'This year we obtained a precious tripod, and this winter's xin-si new moon dawn at winter solstice matches the time of the Yellow Thearch.'

sentences.zh: 卿有札書曰：「黃帝得寶鼎冕候，問於鬼臾區，鬼臾區對曰：『黃帝得寶鼎神策，是歲己酉朔旦冬至，得天之紀，終而復始。』

translations.literal: Qing had a bamboo text that said: 'When the Yellow Thearch obtained a precious tripod and heavenly signs, he asked Guiyuqu. Guiyuqu replied: "The Yellow Thearch obtaining a precious tripod and divine register, in that year ji-you, new moon dawn coincided with winter solstice; he gained Heaven's cycle, ending and beginning again."'
translations.idiomatic: Qing had a bamboo text that said: 'When the Yellow Thearch obtained a precious tripod and heavenly signs, he asked Guiyuqu. Guiyuqu replied: "The Yellow Thearch obtaining a precious tripod and divine register, in that year ji-you, new moon dawn coincided with winter solstice; he gained Heaven's cycle, ending and beginning again."'

sentences.zh: 於是黃帝迎日推策，後率二十歲復朔旦冬至，凡二十推，三百八十年，黃帝僊登于天。」

translations.literal: 'So the Yellow Thearch welcomed the sun and calculated by registers; afterward every twenty years new moon dawn again met winter solstice. After twenty such calculations, over 380 years, the Yellow Thearch transcended and ascended to Heaven.'
translations.idiomatic: 'So the Yellow Thearch welcomed the sun and calculated by registers; afterward every twenty years new moon dawn again met winter solstice. After twenty such calculations, over 380 years, the Yellow Thearch transcended and ascended to Heaven.'

sentences.zh: 卿因所忠欲奏之。

translations.literal: Qing, through Suo Zhong, wished to present this.
translations.idiomatic: Qing, through Suo Zhong, wished to present this.

sentences.zh: 所忠視其書不經，疑其妄言，謝曰：「寶鼎事已決矣。

translations.literal: Suo Zhong looked at the text, found it non-canonical, suspected falsehood, and declined, saying: 'The precious tripod matter is already settled.
translations.idiomatic: Suo Zhong looked at the text, found it non-canonical, suspected falsehood, and declined, saying: 'The precious tripod matter is already settled.

sentences.zh: 尚何以為！」

translations.literal: What more is there to do?'
translations.idiomatic: What more is there to do?'

sentences.zh: 卿因嬖人奏之。

translations.literal: Qing then used a favored court attendant to present it.
translations.idiomatic: Qing then used a favored court attendant to present it.

sentences.zh: 上大說，乃召問卿。

translations.literal: The emperor was greatly pleased and summoned Qing for questioning.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was greatly pleased and summoned Qing for questioning.

sentences.zh: 對曰：「受此書申公，申公已死。」

translations.literal: Qing answered: 'I received this text from Master Shen, who is now dead.'
translations.idiomatic: Qing answered: 'I received this text from Master Shen, who is now dead.'

sentences.zh: 上曰：「申公何人也？」

translations.literal: The emperor asked: 'Who was Master Shen?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked: 'Who was Master Shen?'

sentences.zh: 卿曰：「齊人，與安期生通，受黃帝言，無書，獨有此鼎書。

translations.literal: Qing said: 'A man of Qi, connected with Anqi Sheng. He received sayings of the Yellow Thearch; there are no books, only this tripod text.
translations.idiomatic: Qing said: 'A man of Qi, connected with Anqi Sheng. He received sayings of the Yellow Thearch; there are no books, only this tripod text.

sentences.zh: 曰『漢興復當黃帝之時。』

translations.literal: It says, "When Han rises, it will again be like the age of the Yellow Thearch."
translations.idiomatic: It says, "When Han rises, it will again be like the age of the Yellow Thearch."

sentences.zh: 曰『漢之聖者，在高祖之孫且曾孫也。

translations.literal: It says, "Han's sage ruler will be a grandson or great-grandson of Gaozu.
translations.idiomatic: It says, "Han's sage ruler will be a grandson or great-grandson of Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 寶鼎出而與神通，封禪。

translations.literal: When the precious tripod appears and communication with spirits is achieved, Feng and Shan rites will be performed.
translations.idiomatic: When the precious tripod appears and communication with spirits is achieved, Feng and Shan rites will be performed.

sentences.zh: 封禪七十二王，唯黃帝得上泰山封。』

translations.literal: Of the seventy-two kings who performed Feng and Shan, only the Yellow Thearch truly ascended Mount Tai for Feng."
translations.idiomatic: Of the seventy-two kings who performed Feng and Shan, only the Yellow Thearch truly ascended Mount Tai for Feng."

sentences.zh: 申公曰：『漢帝亦當上封禪，封禪則能僊登天矣。

translations.literal: Master Shen said, "The Han emperor too should ascend for Feng and Shan; with Feng and Shan he can transcend and ascend Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Master Shen said, "The Han emperor too should ascend for Feng and Shan; with Feng and Shan he can transcend and ascend Heaven.

sentences.zh: 黃帝萬諸侯，而神靈之封君七千。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch had ten thousand feudal lords, and among spirit-lords he enfeoffed seven thousand.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch had ten thousand feudal lords, and among spirit-lords he enfeoffed seven thousand.

sentences.zh: 天下名山八，而三在蠻夷，五在中國。

translations.literal: Among the realm's famous mountains there are eight; three are among border peoples and five in the central states.
translations.idiomatic: Among the realm's famous mountains there are eight; three are among border peoples and five in the central states.

sentences.zh: 中國華山、首山、太室山、泰山、東萊山，此五山黃帝之所常游，與神會。

translations.literal: In the central states, Mount Hua, Shoushan, Taishi Mountain, Mount Tai, and Donglai Mountain are the five where the Yellow Thearch often traveled and met spirits.
translations.idiomatic: In the central states, Mount Hua, Shoushan, Taishi Mountain, Mount Tai, and Donglai Mountain are the five where the Yellow Thearch often traveled and met spirits.

sentences.zh: 黃帝且戰且學僊，患百姓非其道，乃斷斬非鬼神者。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch both fought wars and studied transcendence; troubled that common people followed not his way, he cut down those who denied spirits.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch both fought wars and studied transcendence; troubled that common people followed not his way, he cut down those who denied spirits.

sentences.zh: 百餘歲然後得與神通。

translations.literal: Only after more than a hundred years did he attain communication with spirits.
translations.idiomatic: Only after more than a hundred years did he attain communication with spirits.

sentences.zh: 黃帝郊雍上帝，宿三月。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch performed suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch at Yong and lodged there for three months.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch performed suburban sacrifice to the High Thearch at Yong and lodged there for three months.

sentences.zh: 鬼臾區號大鴻，死葬雍，故鴻冢是也。

translations.literal: Guiyuqu was titled Great Hong; when he died he was buried at Yong, and so that is Hong Mound.
translations.idiomatic: Guiyuqu was titled Great Hong; when he died he was buried at Yong, and so that is Hong Mound.

sentences.zh: 其後黃帝接萬靈明庭。

translations.literal: After that, the Yellow Thearch received myriad spirits at Mingtang.
translations.idiomatic: After that, the Yellow Thearch received myriad spirits at Mingtang.

sentences.zh: 明庭者，甘泉也。

translations.literal: Mingtang means Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: Mingtang means Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 所謂寒門者，谷口也。

translations.literal: What is called Cold Gate is Gukou.
translations.idiomatic: What is called Cold Gate is Gukou.

sentences.zh: 黃帝采首山銅，鑄鼎於荊山下。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch mined copper at Shoushan and cast tripods below Jing Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch mined copper at Shoushan and cast tripods below Jing Mountain.

sentences.zh: 鼎既成，有龍垂胡敘下迎黃帝。

translations.literal: After the tripods were completed, a dragon lowered its whiskers to receive the Yellow Thearch.
translations.idiomatic: After the tripods were completed, a dragon lowered its whiskers to receive the Yellow Thearch.

sentences.zh: 黃帝上騎，群臣後宮從上龍七十餘人，龍乃去。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch mounted up; over seventy ministers and palace women followed him onto the dragon, and the dragon then departed.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch mounted up; over seventy ministers and palace women followed him onto the dragon, and the dragon then departed.

sentences.zh: 餘小臣不得上，乃悉持龍敘，龍敘拔，墯，墯黃帝之弓。

translations.literal: Remaining minor ministers could not climb on, so all grabbed the dragon's whiskers; the whiskers broke and fell, along with the Yellow Thearch's bow.
translations.idiomatic: Remaining minor ministers could not climb on, so all grabbed the dragon's whiskers; the whiskers broke and fell, along with the Yellow Thearch's bow.

sentences.zh: 百姓卬望黃帝既上天，乃抱其弓與龍敘號，故後世因名其處曰鼎湖，其弓曰烏號。』」

translations.literal: When people looked up and saw the Yellow Thearch had ascended to Heaven, they embraced his bow and the dragon whiskers and wailed; therefore later generations named that place Dinghu, and that bow was called Wuhao."'
translations.idiomatic: When people looked up and saw the Yellow Thearch had ascended to Heaven, they embraced his bow and the dragon whiskers and wailed; therefore later generations named that place Dinghu, and that bow was called Wuhao."'

sentences.zh: 於是天子曰：「嗟乎！

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven said: 'Ah!
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon the Son of Heaven said: 'Ah!

sentences.zh: 誠得如黃帝，吾視去妻子如脫屣耳。」

translations.literal: If I could truly be like the Yellow Thearch, I would view leaving wives and children as lightly as casting off worn sandals.'
translations.idiomatic: If I could truly be like the Yellow Thearch, I would view leaving wives and children as lightly as casting off worn sandals.'

sentences.zh: 拜卿為郎，使東候神於太室。

translations.literal: He appointed Qing as Gentleman and sent him east to await spirits at Taishi.
translations.idiomatic: He appointed Qing as Gentleman and sent him east to await spirits at Taishi.

sentences.zh: 上遂郊雍，至隴西，登空桐，幸甘泉。

translations.literal: The emperor then held suburban sacrifice at Yong, went to Longxi, climbed Kongtong, and visited Ganquan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then held suburban sacrifice at Yong, went to Longxi, climbed Kongtong, and visited Ganquan.

sentences.zh: 令祠官寬舒等具泰一祠壇，祠壇放亳忌泰一壇，三陔。

translations.literal: He ordered sacrificial officers Kuan Shu and others to prepare a Taiyi altar modeled on Miu Ji's Taiyi altar at Bo, with three tiers.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered sacrificial officers Kuan Shu and others to prepare a Taiyi altar modeled on Miu Ji's Taiyi altar at Bo, with three tiers.

sentences.zh: 五帝壇環居其下，各如其方。

translations.literal: The Five Thearch altars were arranged in a ring below, each corresponding to its proper direction.
translations.idiomatic: The Five Thearch altars were arranged in a ring below, each corresponding to its proper direction.

sentences.zh: 黃帝西南，除八通鬼道。

translations.literal: The Yellow Thearch was in the southwest, with eight spirit pathways cleared.
translations.idiomatic: The Yellow Thearch was in the southwest, with eight spirit pathways cleared.

sentences.zh: 泰一所用，如雍一畤物，而加醴棗脯之屬，殺一氂牛以為俎豆牢具。

translations.literal: What Taiyi used matched the offerings of Yong's first altar, with additions such as sweet ale, dates, and dried meat, and one yak-like ox was slaughtered for the full tripod-and-bowl sacrificial set.
translations.idiomatic: What Taiyi used matched the offerings of Yong's first altar, with additions such as sweet ale, dates, and dried meat, and one yak-like ox was slaughtered for the full tripod-and-bowl sacrificial set.

sentences.zh: 而五帝獨有俎豆醴進。

translations.literal: Only for the Five Thearchs were tripod-and-bowl offerings with ritual ale presented.
translations.idiomatic: Only for the Five Thearchs were tripod-and-bowl offerings with ritual ale presented.

sentences.zh: 其下四方地，為腏，食群神從者及北斗云。

translations.literal: At the lower four directional sectors, minced offerings were made to attendant spirits and to the Northern Dipper.
translations.idiomatic: At the lower four directional sectors, minced offerings were made to attendant spirits and to the Northern Dipper.

sentences.zh: 已祠，胙餘皆燎之。

translations.literal: After sacrifice, all leftover blessed meat was burned.
translations.idiomatic: After sacrifice, all leftover blessed meat was burned.

sentences.zh: 其牛色白，白鹿居其中，彘在鹿中，鹿中水而酒之。

translations.literal: The ox was white; a white deer was placed at its center; a pig was placed within the deer enclosure; water and wine were set with the deer.
translations.idiomatic: The ox was white; a white deer was placed at its center; a pig was placed within the deer enclosure; water and wine were set with the deer.

sentences.zh: 祭日以牛，祭月以羊彘特。

translations.literal: For the sun, one ox was offered; for the moon, one sheep and one pig as single victims.
translations.idiomatic: For the sun, one ox was offered; for the moon, one sheep and one pig as single victims.

sentences.zh: 泰一祝宰則衣紫及繡。

translations.literal: The invocator and steward for Taiyi wore purple and embroidered robes.
translations.idiomatic: The invocator and steward for Taiyi wore purple and embroidered robes.

sentences.zh: 五帝各如其色，日赤，月白。

translations.literal: Each of the Five Thearchs used its own color; the sun used red, the moon white.
translations.idiomatic: Each of the Five Thearchs used its own color; the sun used red, the moon white.

sentences.zh: 十一月辛巳朔旦冬至，昒爽，天子始郊拜泰一。

translations.literal: On the xin-si new moon dawn of the eleventh month, at winter solstice before full light, the Son of Heaven first performed suburban obeisance to Taiyi.
translations.idiomatic: On the xin-si new moon dawn of the eleventh month, at winter solstice before full light, the Son of Heaven first performed suburban obeisance to Taiyi.

sentences.zh: 朝朝日，夕夕月，則揖；

translations.literal: At dawn he faced and greeted the sun; at dusk he faced and greeted the moon, with formal bows.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn he faced and greeted the sun; at dusk he faced and greeted the moon, with formal bows.

sentences.zh: 而見泰一如雍郊禮。

translations.literal: And he audience-paid Taiyi according to the suburban rite of Yong.
translations.idiomatic: And he audience-paid Taiyi according to the suburban rite of Yong.

sentences.zh: 其贊饗曰：「天始以寶鼎神策授皇帝，朔而又朔，終而復始，皇帝敬拜見焉。」

translations.literal: The hymn of offering said: "Heaven first bestowed on the emperor the precious tripod and divine register; new moon after new moon, ending and beginning again - the emperor reverently bows and receives the audience."
translations.idiomatic: The hymn of offering said: "Heaven first bestowed on the emperor the precious tripod and divine register; new moon after new moon, ending and beginning again - the emperor reverently bows and receives the audience."

sentences.zh: 而衣上黃。

translations.literal: He wore upper garments of yellow.
translations.idiomatic: He wore upper garments of yellow.

sentences.zh: 其祠列火滿壇，壇旁亨炊具。

translations.literal: At that sacrifice, fires were arrayed all around the altar, and cooking vessels were set beside it.
translations.idiomatic: At that sacrifice, fires were arrayed all around the altar, and cooking vessels were set beside it.

sentences.zh: 有司云「祠上有光」。

translations.literal: Officials said, "There was light above the sacrifice."
translations.idiomatic: Officials said, "There was light above the sacrifice."

sentences.zh: 公卿言「皇帝始郊見泰一雲陽，有司奉瑄玉嘉牲薦饗，是夜有美光，及晝，黃氣上屬天。」

translations.literal: The high ministers said, "When the emperor first made suburban audience to Taiyi at Yunyang, officials offered fine jade and auspicious victims; that night there was beautiful light, and by day yellow qi rose and joined the sky."
translations.idiomatic: The high ministers said, "When the emperor first made suburban audience to Taiyi at Yunyang, officials offered fine jade and auspicious victims; that night there was beautiful light, and by day yellow qi rose and joined the sky."

sentences.zh: 太史令談、祠官寬舒等曰：「神靈之休，祐福兆祥，宜因此地光域立泰畤壇以明應。

translations.literal: Grand Astrologer Tan and sacrificial officer Kuan Shu said: "This was divine favor, blessing, and auspicious sign. At this place of radiance, a Tai altar should be established to make the response manifest.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Astrologer Tan and sacrificial officer Kuan Shu said: "This was divine favor, blessing, and auspicious sign. At this place of radiance, a Tai altar should be established to make the response manifest.

sentences.zh: 令太祝領，秋及臘間祠。

translations.literal: Let the Grand Invocator oversee it, with sacrifices in autumn and before year-end.
translations.idiomatic: Let the Grand Invocator oversee it, with sacrifices in autumn and before year-end.

sentences.zh: 二歲天子壹郊見。」

translations.literal: Every second year the Son of Heaven should personally make suburban audience."
translations.idiomatic: Every second year the Son of Heaven should personally make suburban audience."

sentences.zh: 其秋，為伐南越，告禱泰一，以牡荊畫幡日月北斗登龍，以象太一三星，為泰一ⓑ旗，命曰「靈旗」。

translations.literal: That autumn, for the campaign against Nanyue, they reported and prayed to Taiyi, making a sacred banner painted with sun, moon, northern dipper, and ascending dragon on male-jing wood, symbolizing Taiyi's three stars, and named it the Numinous Banner.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, for the campaign against Nanyue, they reported and prayed to Taiyi, making a sacred banner painted with sun, moon, northern dipper, and ascending dragon on male-jing wood, symbolizing Taiyi's three stars, and named it the Numinous Banner.

sentences.zh: 為兵禱，則太史奉以指所伐國。

translations.literal: When praying for war, the Grand Astrologer carried it and pointed toward the state to be attacked.
translations.idiomatic: When praying for war, the Grand Astrologer carried it and pointed toward the state to be attacked.

sentences.zh: 而五利將軍使不敢入海，之泰山祠。

translations.literal: General Five Benefits then did not dare enter the sea and instead went to sacrifice at Mount Tai.
translations.idiomatic: General Five Benefits then did not dare enter the sea and instead went to sacrifice at Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 上使人隨驗，實無所見。

translations.literal: The emperor sent people to verify him, but they truly saw nothing.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent people to verify him, but they truly saw nothing.

sentences.zh: 五利妄言見其師，其方盡，多不讎。

translations.literal: Five Benefits falsely claimed to have seen his master; his methods were exhausted, and most did not come true.
translations.idiomatic: Five Benefits falsely claimed to have seen his master; his methods were exhausted, and most did not come true.

sentences.zh: 上乃誅五利。

translations.literal: So the emperor executed Five Benefits.
translations.idiomatic: So the emperor executed Five Benefits.

sentences.zh: 其冬，公孫卿候神河南，言見僊人跡緱氏城上，有物如雉，往來城上。

translations.literal: That winter, Gongsun Qing was waiting for spirits in Henan and said he saw traces of transcendents above Goushi city, with something like a pheasant coming and going over the walls.
translations.idiomatic: That winter, Gongsun Qing was waiting for spirits in Henan and said he saw traces of transcendents above Goushi city, with something like a pheasant coming and going over the walls.

sentences.zh: 天子親幸緱氏視跡，問卿：「得毋效文成、五利乎？」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven personally went to Goushi to inspect the traces and asked Qing: "Are you not repeating Wencheng and Five Benefits?"
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven personally went to Goushi to inspect the traces and asked Qing: "Are you not repeating Wencheng and Five Benefits?"

sentences.zh: 卿曰：「僊者非有求人主，人主者求之。

translations.literal: Qing replied: "Transcendents do not seek rulers; rulers seek them.
translations.idiomatic: Qing replied: "Transcendents do not seek rulers; rulers seek them.

sentences.zh: 其道非少寬暇，神不來。

translations.literal: Without ample leisure and patience in the method, spirits do not come.
translations.idiomatic: Without ample leisure and patience in the method, spirits do not come.

sentences.zh: 言神事，如迂誕，積以歲，乃可致。」

translations.literal: Talk of divine matters sounds absurd and extravagant, but only over accumulated years can they be brought."
translations.idiomatic: Talk of divine matters sounds absurd and extravagant, but only over accumulated years can they be brought."

sentences.zh: 於是郡國各除道，繕治宮館名山神祠所，以望幸矣。

translations.literal: Thus each commandery and kingdom cleared roads and repaired palaces, lodges, and shrines of famous mountains in expectation of an imperial visit.
translations.idiomatic: Thus each commandery and kingdom cleared roads and repaired palaces, lodges, and shrines of famous mountains in expectation of an imperial visit.

sentences.zh: 其春，既滅南越，嬖臣李延年以好音見。

translations.literal: That spring, after Nanyue was destroyed, the favored retainer Li Yannian gained audience through fine music.
translations.idiomatic: That spring, after Nanyue was destroyed, the favored retainer Li Yannian gained audience through fine music.

sentences.zh: 上善之，下公卿議，曰：「民間祠有鼓舞樂，今郊祀而無樂，豈稱乎？」
